searchText: Arm Care Pitch Count Rules Faq Arm Care & Pitch Count Rules FAQ Arm Care & Pitch Count Rule FAQ Skip to main content Arm Care & Pitch Count Rules FAQ – Arm Care COACHING CENTRE UMPIRE ZONE FEMALE BASEBALL ARM CARE MORE SIGN IN / REGISTER Arm Care & Pitch Count Rules FAQ Updated for the 2026 baseball season Arm Care & Pitch Count Rule FAQ "How do I..." - A full guide to the Pitch Count App Where can I find information on the Arm Care rules? What are the pitch count limits and days rest requirements for each division? What happens when a pitcher reaches their limit? Can pitchers pitch on multiple games on the same day? Can pitchers pitch two days in a row? Can pitchers pitch three days in a row? Can a player pitch and catch in the same game/day? Who is responsible for entering the pitch count and when? Do pitch counts have to be entered live in-game, or can they be entered after the game? What if I don't have Wi-Fi or data at the park? What should the home team do if either team has reached their pitch count limit? What are my obligations when traveling outside of Ontario or Canada? What penalties apply when a team violates the pitch count rules? What happens when a team is not in the app? What happens when a player is not in the app? There's an error on my roster in the app, how can I fix it? How do I submit a trouble ticket for the Pitch Count app? "HOW DO I...?" A FULL GUIDE TO THE PITCH COUNT APP Have you ever wondered how to do something on the Pitch Count app? OBA has created a full series of How-To videos and Frequently Asked Questions on using the Pitch Count App. Please visit coachoba.ca for all the how-to and FAQ you will need! [TOP] WHERE CAN I FIND INFORMATION ON THE ARM CARE RULES? The full Arm Care rules can be found in the Baseball Ontario ONDeck app in the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules under Section A12. OBA Playing Rules [TOP] WHAT ARE THE PITCH COUNT LIMITS AND DAYS REST REQUIREMENTS FOR EACH DIVISION? Day 1 of the rest period is the day after the game the pitcher last pitched. Days rest is calculated based on the total number of pitches thrown. For example, if an 11U pitcher throws 65 pitches on Friday, their days rest would be 3-days and they would be permitted to next pitch again on Tuesday. "Last Batter" can be declared prior to the threshold being reached and any pitches thrown after the threshold are not counted. Example: The 15U threshold for zero days rest is 35 pitches. The pitcher begins the new batter at 32 pitches. Prior to the 35th pitch, the Manager indicates that this shall be the last batter for the pitcher. The pitcher is permitted to complete the current batter and a final pitch count of 35 is recorded. [TOP] WHAT HAPPENS WHEN A PITCHER REACHES THEIR LIMIT? When a pitcher reaches their pitch count limit during a game, and they are in the middle of an at-bat, the pitcher is permitted to continue to pitch until that batter is retired (under most circumstances). Please see Can Pitchers Pitch Three Days in a Row? below for the exception to this rule. When a pitcher reaches the maximum number of pitches allowed for any days-rest threshold, they may complete the at-bat without affecting their days rest, as long as they do not pitch to another batter. In this situation, the number of pitches corresponding to the threshold will be indicated on the pitching log (not the actual pitch count). The coach or manager must acknowledge this with the umpire prior to the threshold pitch being thrown. The umpire would then notify the official scorekeeper. Example: The 15U threshold for zero days rest is 35 pitches. The pitcher begins the new batter at 32 pitches. Prior to the 35th pitch, the Manager indicates that this shall be the last batter for the pitcher. The pitcher is permitted to complete the current batter and a final pitch count of 35 is recorded. At the end of the at-bat, the pitcher must be removed from the pitching position. [TOP] CAN PITCHERS PITCH IN MULTIPLE GAMES ON THE SAME DAY? Yes, pitchers can pitch in 2 games (max) on the same day as long as they don’t exceed their no rest required threshold in the first game of the day. The pitch count for both games will be added together to calculate days rest required. For example, a 15U pitcher could throw 25 pitches in game 1 of a doubleheader and in game 2 can pitch again because they haven’t exceeded their no-days rest threshold. They start game 2 at pitch 26 and can throw a total of 95 pitches combined over the two games. [TOP] CAN PITCHERS PITCH TWO DAYS IN A ROW? Yes, pitchers can pitch on two consecutive days as long as they haven’t exceeded their no rest required threshold on the first day and have only pitched in one game on the first day. On Day 2, the pitcher starts the day with the same number of pitches thrown on Day 1. So, for example, if a pitcher threw 20 pitches on Friday, they can pitch again on Saturday but their pitch count for the day starts at 21, not at zero. Pitchers can not pitch in more than 2 games in any 2-day period. In all cases, the pitch count for all games are added together to calculate days rest required. [TOP] CAN PITCHERS PITCH THREE DAYS IN A ROW? Yes, pitchers can pitch on three consecutive days as long as they do not exceed their no rest required threshold in all three games combined. For example, at 13U, a pitcher could throw 10 pitches each day on Friday, Saturday, and Sunday; as long as the pitcher does not exceed 30 pitches over the three consecutive days, this is permitted. If a pitcher throws on three consecutive days, they must rest for 1 day before they are eligible to pitch again. [TOP] CAN A PLAYER PITCH AND CATCH IN THE SAME GAME/DAY? No. Players can not pitch and catch in the same day regardless of which position they started in. Once a player has assumed the pitching position, they are no longer permitted to catch for the remainder of the day. Once a player has assumed the catching position, they are no longer permitted to pitch for the remainder of the day. [TOP] WHO IS RESPONSIBLE FOR ENTERING THE PITCH COUNT? The Home Team is always responsible for entering the pitch count for both teams. The Head Coach is accountable for the pitch count and responsible to ensure that it is entered. However, the coach may delegate the pitch count to a trusted assistant coach, team manager, trainer, or other volunteer. [TOP] WHEN DO PITCH COUNTS HAVE TO BE ENTERED? DO PITCH COUNTS HAVE TO BE ENTERED LIVE IN-GAME OR CAN THEY BE ENTERED AFTER THE GAME? In Rep and Select tournaments, pitch counts must be entered in the app in real-time during the game (either pitch-by-pitch or at the end of each half-inning). During regular season play it is permitted to enter pitch counts after the game as long as they are entered within 24 hours. However, OBA strongly recommends pitch-by-pitch tracking in the app for all games to ensure that coaches are notified of potential violations before they occur to avoid penalties to their team. Pitch-by-pitch tracking also displays real-time pitch counts, pitches remaining, and dramatically reduces the volume of data entry errors versus entering pitch counts post-game. [TOP] WHAT IF I DON'T HAVE ACCESS TO WI-FI OR DATA AT THE PARK? The Pitch Count app works without Wi-Fi or mobile data. Simply track your pitches as you normally would and submit the final pitch count as soon as you reconnect to Wi-Fi or data. The app uses minimal data since it's not transmitting large amounts of information. [TOP] WHAT SHOULD THE HOME TEAM DO IF EITHER TEAM HAS REACHED THEIR PITCH COUNT LIMIT? If pitch counts are being recorded pitch-by-pitch, the pitch counter will receive alerts, the screen will turn ORANGE on their device when a player is approaching and has reached their maximum pitch count. As it is the Home team's responsibility to capture the count, it is also the responsibility for the Home team's Manager/Head Coach to notify the plate umpire when the opposing team’s pitcher has reached their pitch count limit as soon as the screen on the app turns RED. The umpire must notify the Away Team Head Coach. The Head Coach is responsible for reporting to the umpire regardless of if they have delegated the pitch count responsibilities to someone. The Away team has the right to request the pitch count for either team at any time throughout the game to ensure that the count is accurate, and rules have not been violated. [TOP] WHAT ARE MY OBLIGATIONS WHEN TRAVELING OUTSIDE OF ONTARIO OR CANADA? All Arm Care rules are mandatory when traveling outside Ontario or Canada. Games played in US or other out-of-province territories must be entered into Pitch Count with no opponent selected within 24 hours after the completion of each game. Sanctions apply for coaches who fail to report Pitch Counts, exceed thresholds, or do not follow required Days Rest periods while playing games out of province. [TOP] WHAT PENALTIES APPLY WHEN A TEAM VIOLATES THE PITCH COUNT RULES? Different penalties apply depending on which team violates the pitch count rule, whether or not the umpire was notified, as well as if and when the violation was reported. TEAM VIOLATION VIOLATION REPORTED PENALTY Home Team violates pitch count In game Forfeit + 1 game suspension for Head Coach Home Team violates pitch count Post-game No forfeit 2 game suspension for Head Coach Away Team violates pitch count after notification by Official Pitch Counter to umpire at time of infraction In game Forfeit + 1 game suspension for Head Coach Away Team violates pitch count with NO notification to umpire prior to the time of infraction In game No forfeit Head Coach is ejected + 1 game suspension for Head Coach Away team violates pitch count Post-game No forfeit 2 game suspension for Head Coach IMPORTANT NOTE ON ENFORCEMENT: The intent of these rules is to enforce the pitch count rules and not to promote forfeits. The Home Team is responsible for the pitch count and therefore the duty of responsibility falls upon them to notify the opposing team of a pitcher reaching their pitch count via the plate umpire. Regardless of whether or not, the Home Team Head Coach delegates the pitch count to someone else, they are ultimately responsible and accountable to the Pitch Count. [TOP] WHAT HAPPENS IF A TEAM IS NOT IN THE APP? If a team is not in the app, it means that their roster has not yet been submitted and the team is not permitted to play. After May 1st, any team playing without having submitted a roster shall be automatically suspended until their roster is submitted. [TOP] WHAT HAPPENS IF A PLAYER IS NOT ON THE TEAM IN THE APP? If a player is not on the team in the app, they are not permitted to pitch. Call-up players can be added only by the team they are playing on before the game starts. Once the game starts, they can’t be added to the team in the app and are therefore not allowed to pitch. [TOP] THERE'S AN ERROR ON MY ROSTER, HOW CAN I FIX IT? Pitch Count rosters are pulled directly from the BaseballShift rostering system. If there is an error on your roster in Pitch Count, it is highly likely the result of incorrect data on your BaseballShift roster. Please reach out to your Local Registrar to have your roster corrected. Some common issues include: Missing player: This is a result of the player either not being entered into your BaseballShift roster, or the player does not have a valid birthdate on the roster. Incorrect days rest applied: This is usually the result of a player having an incorrect birthdate on the roster. Caution should be taken when selecting an existing player in the rostering system to reduce duplication of players on multiple teams. Missing or wrong coach: Coaches are required to have their email address and NCCP number on their roster. Pitch Counter / Team Manager Not On App: Only staff entered with valid email addresses will be given access to the app. If you wish your Team Manager to manage Pitch Counts for you, you must select Pitch Counter in the drop down menu on the BaseballShift roster. Note: this gives any staff full access to your Pitch Count app. I'm a coach and my name appears on my team in the app, but I haven't received my login: It is likely that your email address has been entered incorrectly on your roster (or a different one than you're using). Please see below for information on submitting a trouble ticket to Pitch Count Support. [TOP] HOW DO I SUBMIT A TROUBLE TICKET FOR THE PITCH COUNT APP? If you encounter a bug, have feedback on the app, or have a question for Pitch Count Support, please email pitchcountapp@baseballontario.com BEFORE YOU EMAIL: Be sure to email from the email address associated with your Pitch Count app if possible. Always include your Team ID which can be found in the URL for your BaseballShift roster (see below). If your question is in regard to a specific game, please include the game number. If your question is in regard to a specific player or coach, please include their full name. [TOP] Last Updated: 29-July-2024 SearchMember DirectoryEventsEmail Support Copyright © 2026 New Start Mobile, LLC https://ondeck.baseballontario.com/page/784/arm-care/16475/arm-care-pitch-count-rules-faq

Guidelines for Affiliated Associations Appendix B - Affiliated Associations Appendix C - National Championship Player Drafts RP1.1 Age and Category To be eligible to play in a particular age category, a player must not have a birthday which makes them older than the number in the series name. ie: A player in 9U (9 and Under) must not turn 10 dururing the current calendar year. CATEGORY/SERIES AGE OF PLAYER AS OF DECEMBER 31st OF THE CALENDAR YEAR 8U Not older than 8 years of age but not younger than 6 as of December 31st 9U Not older than 9 years of age 10U Not older than 10 years of age 11U Not older than 11 years of age 12U Not older than 12 years of age 13U/13U Girls Not older than 13 years of age 14U Not older than 14 years of age 15U Not older than 15 years of age 16U/16U Girls Not older than 16 years of age 18U Not older than 18 years of age 22U Not older than 22 years of age Senior/Women’s Unlimited but not younger than 17 as of December 31st [TOP] RP1.2 Birth Records All players 21 years of age and under must produce a birth record the first time they register with OBA. Only birth certificates, passports, citizenship cards or driver’s licenses will be accepted as birth records. In the case of Affiliated Associations having their own registrar and having the approval of the OBA, it is not necessary to send birth records to the OBA Office if such birth records are provided to the Affiliated Association’s registrar and will be recorded by the registrar. For Affiliated Associations not having their own registrar or for Direct Entries, when sending birth records to the OBA Office, they must be listed, and a copy of the list included with the birth records. Upon receipt of a birth record, it will be recorded in the OBA Office. Players who cannot procure a birth record may sign a team roster upon producing a statutory declaration sworn by their parent or guardian establishing their age; the same to be accompanied by a certificate of a solicitor or notary public. [TOP] RP1.3 Residency Rules The basic premise of the Rep Division’s residence rule is that players are to play with the Local Association where they reside. Players must establish residence within the Local Association on or before June 30th of the playing year. Exception: a player, who (1) maintains Canadian citizenship, (2) has been a member of a Rep Local Association or Direct Entry for at least one year prior, (3) continues to be a member of the same Rep Local Association or Direct Entry, and (4) for any reason is living outside of the Province of Ontario, may continue to register with that Local Association or Direct Entry. Individuals may be granted permission by the Rep Registrar to participate in Rep play. Cases will be dealt with on an individual basis (i.e., exchange students). Affiliated Associations must establish territorial boundaries for Local Associations within the Affiliated Association. Affiliated Associations may also set policies applicable within their Affiliated Association. A copy of the boundaries and any related policies must be filed with Baseball Ontario. The Rep Division will consider the most recent copies filed by the Affiliated Association in any dispute. The Rep Division may, in its discretion, choose to not follow the Affiliated Association policy where it conflicts with the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules and/or the Procedures and Playing Rules of the Rep Division or the best interests of Baseball Ontario. In areas where no Local Association exists, the Affiliated Association may set policy. In the absence of such a policy, The Rep Division shall apply the following policy: The player may play for any team in the Affiliated Association without a release. In areas where there is a Local Association, but the Local Association does not have a team at the particular series, the Affiliated Association may set policy. In the absence of such a policy, The Rep Division shall apply the following policy: The player may play for any team in the Affiliated Association without a release. For the purposes of this rule, minor (e.g., 8U, 10U, 12U, 14U, 16U) is not defined as a separate series from major (e.g., 9U, 11U, 13U, 15U, 18U). If a combined minor/major program is offered by a Local Association, then the Local Association is considered to have a team at the particular series. The player is expected to return to his/her Local Association, unless a released is obtained pursuant to RP1.5. A player in the circumstances of e) or f) may play for a team in another Affiliated Association without a release where such team is the team closest to his/her place of residence. Closest team shall be interpreted as the distance from the player’s home to the relevant teams’ home baseball diamond as the crow flies. Where a Local Association has more than 1 team operating in the same series, the Local Association shall set policy and/or boundaries for such teams. The Local Association shall file a copy of such policy and/or boundaries with the Affiliated Association and Baseball Ontario. [TOP] RP1.4 Players Changing Residence Baseball Ontario recognizes that families move. As set out in RP1.3b), players must establish residence prior to June 30th of the playing season. In split family situations (i.e., the parents are divorced or separated), players 18 years of age and younger shall be deemed to reside where the parent(s) having custody rights reside. Baseball Ontario does not condone moves of convenience or falsified family splits designed to bypass the release process. Baseball Ontario, when considering the legitimacy of the family move or family split, may have regard to a number of factor(s), including, but not limited to the following: a statement from the parent(s) outlining that they actually live at the stated address; a copy of a rental agreement or proof of ownership of the residence; a driver’s license with the stated address; the name and address of the school the player attends; a separation agreement or divorce decree outlining the custody and access arrangements of the player; and/or Canada Revenue Agency Marital Status Change Form – RC 65 [TOP] RP1.5 Players Changing Teams, Releases, and Transfers Players are only allowed to be registered with one team at any time. Players registered with one team must be properly released before registering with a second team. This applies to all series. A player’s playing rights are held by: (i) the Local Association(s) where they reside as set forth in RP1.3 and RP1.4; and (ii) the Local Association where they played the previous season if the player obtained all necessary releases under this section RP1.5. At the Affiliated Association level, a player’s playing rights are held by the Affiliated Association(s) where they reside as set forth RP1.3 and RP1.4. Before a player may participate in a practice, game, tryout, evaluation, or other baseball activity with a team that does not currently hold the player’s playing rights, the player must obtain the appropriate authorization as specified below. Where a player wishes to participate in another team’s out-of-province game or tournament and is registered with the Local Association that holds their playing rights, then permission from the Local Association and the Affiliated Association are required. Where a player desires to register with a Direct Entry or Local Association that does not hold their playing rights, the player must obtain: a release from each Local Association that hold their playing rights as set out in RP1.5 b); and a release from the Affiliated Association where the player resides, if they desire to register with a Direct Entry or Local Association outside of the Affiliated Association where they reside. Note: The request for a release must be in writing and the granting or refusal of a release shall be in writing authorized by the current management of the Direct Entry, Local Association or Affiliated Association involved. The foregoing does not apply at AAAA, 22U, or Senior where no release is required, and a player may sign with a team that does not hold their playing rights without a release as long as there is no equipment, uniform or financial obligation owing to their previous team. Where a player desires to participate in a tryout, evaluation, practice, or other baseball activity with a team that does not currently hold the player’s playing rights, the player must obtain the releases required by RP1.5e). Release requests must be responded to and adjudicated by the Local Association or Affiliated Association, as the case may be, within 14 days of receipt. The Local Association or Affiliated Association may extend this due date by an additional 14 days should more information be required and must do so in writing to the person requesting the release. Should the request not be responded to within these parameters, the release will be automatically denied. Where a Local Association or Direct Entry grants a player a release, such release is valid and remains in full force and effect so long as the player continues to play for the Local Association or Direct Entry with which the player registered on the basis of having been granted such release. All releases must be in writing and cannot be conditional. Any player, for whom a Local Association is not prepared to offer a spot on one of the Local Association’s OBA teams, is deemed to be released. The Local Association is required to provide a written release upon request by the player. Any player, who has not been played by his/her team in 3 or more consecutive games, has the right to appeal to the OBA Technical Committee should the player’s team refuse to release the player. Releases shall not be valid until filed with the OBA Office. [TOP] RP1.6 Release Appeals Where a player has been denied a release under RP1.5 by a Local Association, the player has the right to appeal in writing the Local Association’s refusal to the Affiliated Association. The Affiliated Association will make a decision to grant or deny the release. The Affiliated Association’s decision is to be in writing. Either the player or the Local Association may appeal the Affiliated Association’s decision to the OBA Technical Committee. No appeal may be made until the Affiliated Association has made its decision. The appeal must include all evidence and correspondence submitted to the Local Association and/or the Affiliated Association and copies of all decisions made by the Local Association and Affiliated Association. The OBA Technical Committee will establish the time and place of the appeal hearing, which will be held within 30 days of receiving the written appeal and all documents required in RP1.6 c). The player (and/or his/her parent(s) or guardian(s)) and the Direct Entry or Local Association and/or Affiliated Association must be present in person at the hearing to provide evidence in support of their position. Failure to appear may result in a default decision. The decision of the OBA Technical Committee shall be final. [TOP] RP1.7 Offenses Where a player participates in a practice, game, tryout, evaluation, or other baseball activity with a team that does not currently hold the player’s playing rights without the authorization(s) required by RP1.5, the player is subject to suspension by OBA. Any Head Coach, Assistant Coach or manager who allows a player to participate in any practice, game, tryout, evaluation, or other baseball activity with a team that does not currently hold the player’s playing rights without the authorization(s) required by RP1.5, is subject to suspension by OBA. No player, Head Coach, Assistant Coach or manager may approach any player for whom their Local Association or Direct Entry does not hold the playing rights with the intention of securing said player for their Local Association or Direct Entry. Violation of this rule will leave the player, coach, or manager subject to suspension by OBA. Where a director, officer, employee or other member of a Local Association or Direct Entry (who is not a player, Head Coach, Assistant Coach or manager) approaches any player for whom their Local Association or Direct Entry does not hold the playing rights with the intention of securing said player for their Local Association or Direct Entry, then such violation will leave the Local Association or Direct Entry open to a fine of up to $1000. In addition to or in place of any suspension authorized by this section RP1.7, any person or organization violating any of the provisions of section RP1.7 may be subject to fine or other sanction as determined appropriate by the Technical Committee. [TOP] RP1.8 Advancing to a Higher Series A player registered with a team in any series 22U or below shall be allowed to advance to a higher series of their own Local Association without affecting their status in the series and classification of the team with which they are registered. Any player, who advances, must play with their original team when the team they advance to and the team they are registered with have games on the same date. Where the original (rostered) team is competing in a weekend tournament, the player shall not be eligible to be used as a call up to the team they are advancing to until their original (rostered) team is eliminated from competition in their tournament. (Note: Any team knowingly postponing a game so that a player may advance will be suspended from the Rep Provincial Championships.) (Local Association shall be interpreted as the Local Association with which the player is registered.) In advancing under RP1.8 a), a player may only advance as follows: A 9U (including 8U) player may only advance to 11U. A 11U (including 10U) may advance only to 13U. A 13U (including 12U) may advance only to 15U. A 15U(including 14U) may advance only to 18U. Players who turn 16 years of age in the calendar year may only advance to 22U. For the 15U and 18U National Qualifiers only, OBA teams may call players across from other teams within their Local Association at the same age category, so long as that team is not also playing in the National Qualifier (e.g., an 18U team may call a player to their National Qualifier team from their 17U team (i.e. an 18U team of all 17U aged players), or their 18U A team). For greater clarity, a player may only play for one team in any one National Qualifier tournament. Where a Local Association has more than one team at a series, players that are on the lower classified team may be “called up” to the higher classified team. Where a Local Association has both a Select Division team(s) and a Rep team(s) at a series, players from the Select Division team(s) may be called up to the Rep team(s), provided the players comply with the residency rules and are the proper age. Where a player of a younger age is rostered with a team at an older age category (e.g., A 13U aged player is rostered on a 15U roster), such player shall be considered as a player of the older series at which he/she is rostered despite being of younger age and shall not be eligible to play with the younger team. Where a team wishes to use a call up player from a team that has been eliminated from any further competition during a tournament or a Rep Provincial Championship tournament or a Rep National Qulaifier tournament being held the same weekend, must have either the pitch count records or copies of the official game sheets for all games involving the call-up player. Any player, for whom the pitching eligibility cannot be confirmed, shall not be eligible to pitch. Once discovered, the penalty for violating any provision of RP1.8 shall be forfeiture by the team to which the player advances. The Series Chair shall determine what other appropriate steps will be taken in the circumstances. The Series Chair's decision is final and is not appealable. [TOP] RP1.9 Player Registration Each year, Local Associations will register their players and teams with the OBA through an online registration platform in accordance with policies and procedures determined by the OBA. The OBA will publish such policies and procedures on its website. By virtue of submitting a roster, the Local Association accepts responsibility for the authenticity of the information contained on the roster. The process may commence by a date determined by Rep Council which shall be no later than October 1st annually. Teams must submit a preliminary roster to the OBA Office through the OBA online registration platform no later than April 28th annually or five business days prior to the team's first game, whichever is later. Any team playing without having submitted a roster after May 1st shall be automatically suspended until such time as the roster is submitted. Rosters may be edited by Local Associations until May 31st. No changes to players on a roster may be made after May 31st without the consent of the OBA Rep Commissioner. At 19U Women’s, Senior Women’s, and Senior, submitted rosters may be edited by Local Associations until June 30th. At 19U Women’s, Senior Women’s, and Senior, no changes to players on a roster may be made after June 30th without the consent of the OBA Rep Registrar. At a minimum, the information to be provided through such online registration system will include: Name Date of birth Gender Address of residence (including postal code) Telephone number, and Valid email address Affiliated Associations and the OBA may review submitted rosters to ensure compliance with requirements of player registration and coach certification. Affiliated Associations and the OBA may remove players and coaches not meeting requirements from rosters. Teams with rosters not meeting the requirements of player registration and coach certification are not eligible to participate in Rep Provincial Championships and/or Rep National Qualifier Tournaments. No player may participate in a Rep tournament or Rep Provincial Championship without their name on a team roster. If a player, who is not officially on the team roster or on the roster of a team from which the team may call up players, participates in a game, once it is discovered, the Head Coach will receive a 10-game suspension. The suspension is not appealable. Subject to Baseball Canada eligibility rules, Senior teams attending Baseball Canada Men’s Nationals may add Intercounty Baseball League players as call-ups to their roster for Nationals. [TOP] RP1.10 Team Number A team must have 9 signed players and may have a maximum of 25 players who must be signed to a team roster of the series of the team entered. At 22U and Senior, the maximum roster size is 30 players. A maximum of 25 players can appear on a line-up card for any one game. Each team must have at least 1 manager, and/or 1 or more coaches, signed to the roster clearly marked “Manager” or “Coach” and who shall be responsible for that team. [TOP] RP1.11 Professional Players Registration Any player signed by a professional baseball club of Major League Baseball (MLB) or Minor League Baseball (MiLB), who has received his/her unconditional release or has been placed on the Voluntary Retired List by said professional club before June 30th of the playing season shall become eligible for reinstatement to play in the OBA immediately when he/she returns to his/her home city or town, or to the team he/she left to turn professional or he/she may play with any other team providing he/she qualifies under the residence rule. All applications for reinstatement must be accompanied by a letter from the Office of the President of the MiLB or a copy of the release from the applicable professional organization. Any player signed by an independent professional league or club who has received his/her unconditional release or has been placed on the voluntary retired list by said independent professional club who provides a written statement advising that they no longer play in that league before June 30th of the playing season shall become eligible for reinstatement to play in the OBA immediately when he/she returns to his/her home city or town, or to the team he/she left to turn professional or he/she may play with any other team providing he/she qualifies under the residence rule. A player shall be considered to have turned professional when he/she plays his/her first game, either exhibition or league, with a professional club. The release, voluntary retirement confirmation or written statement must accompany any application for reinstatement. [TOP] RP1.12 Eligibility Players are not eligible to register with the OBA for the then current playing season if they have registered with a non-recognized baseball program operating in the Province of Ontario for any part of such playing season. Players registered with the OBA for the then current playing season who register with a non-recognized baseball program shall be subject to suspension in accordance with section RP7.1. Individual players may participate with a non-recognized program outside of the province if they secure letters of permission from: (1) Rep Head Coach, (2) Local Association Executive, and (3) Affiliate Association Executive; allowing them to play with a specified team or organization. (All letters and information shall be forwarded to the OBA through the Affiliated Association). A player participating with a non-recognized program outside of the province without these approvals shall be automatically suspended indefinitely. Any further penalty shall be determined by the OBA Rep Council. Head Coaches, Assistant Coaches, managers, Rep Council members, Local Association and Affiliated Association Board Members shall immediately disqualify themselves for participation within OBA's Rep Division, its Affiliated Associations and Local Associations, if they are directly involved in any way with a non-recognized baseball program within the Province of Ontario. OBA-carded umpires are not permitted to umpire non-recognized baseball program games. For clarity, umpires are always permitted to umpire any game that involves at least one OBA program. Umpires who violate this rule are subject to immediate and indefinite suspension, pending review. Teams from non-recognized programs may not enter an OBA-sanctioned tournament. For the purpose of this section, the following terms shall have the following meanings: “non-recognized baseball program” shall mean any baseball program other than (1) a baseball program operated exclusively by the OBA, its Affiliated Associations, and its Local Associations, (2) a high school, college or university baseball program, (3) a baseball program operated exclusively by an associated member of the OBA, (4) a baseball program recognized by the Rep Council, or (5) a baseball program operated by an HP Independent Program in accordance with Section RP2.13 playing season” shall mean the period from April 15 through to the end of the Labour Day weekend. “registered” includes any form of signing or registration process and any player participating on a regular basis with a non-recognized baseball program shall be deemed to have been registered with such program. A player participating in another recognized program, including an HP Independent Program, shall have the right to sign only with: the Local Association in whose territory the player lives, or the Local Association with which the player was last rostered. In the case of a player participating with an HP Independent Program, in addition to the above, the Rep team with which the player signs must be classified at AA or below. Despite Section RP1.8 d. and e., a player with an HP Independent Program rostered on a Rep team AA or below may not be used as a call up to a Rep AAA team or an HP-OBA team. To be eligible to participate in a Rep Play-off, a player participating with an HP Independent Program must have participated in 60% of the team’s league games. If they have not, then the player is ineligible to participate in the Rep Play-off. OBA will enforce this rule on the basis of a challenge to a player’s eligibility and not proactively. If there is a challenge, the team will need to provide evidence of the player’s eligibility. Teams may apply to the Rep Commissioner for an exemption from the 60% requirement for extenuating circumstances (e.g. injury). The Rep Commissioner, in consultation with the Rep Deputy Commissioner, shall rule on the exemption request. Such determination is final. If the player is not signed by such Local Association, then he/she shall not be entitled to sign with another Local Association. Senior Players are exempt from RP1.12 g) [TOP] RP1.13 High Performance Programs There shall be two types of High Performance Programs recognized by OBA: HP OBA PROGRAMS (HP-OBA) Requirements: To register as an HP-OBA Program, the program must: be operated by a Local Association or Affiliated Association and roster with OBA through OBA’s approved platform, including paying applicable fees through its Affiliated Association; adhere to the age categories approved by Baseball Canada; ensure all coaches for each team obtain and maintain the minimum coaching standards for their age category for a AAA coach as published by OBA and for nationals as published by Baseball Canada; follow OBA Arm Care rules and use the OBA Pitch Count app in all games throughout the season. Opportunities: HP-OBA Programs may: compete in the Rep National Qualifier Tournament, if applicable; actively promote their try-outs on social media, websites or other public advertisements at any time; at 12U and above, sign players without the need to follow the release requirements of RP1.5 and hold early try-outs, subject to the timelines identified below. Timelines: To take advantage of the no release requirement and early try-out opportunities for the following season, an HP-OBA Program must: declare their intention to register as an HP-OBA Program for the following season by May 1 annually, otherwise the release requirements of RP1.5 would apply; not engage in recruitment or hold try-outs prior to: July 1 in the case of teams operating at 12U or 13U in the following season; or May 1 in the case of teams operating at 14U and above in the following season; complete their try-outs and announce their roster for the following season’s team by the HP-OBA Roster Deadline of: November 1 for teams operating at 12U or 13U in the following season; or The Wednesday after Labour Day for teams operating at 14U and above in the following season. For purposes of these timelines, “recruitment” is defined as any solicitation of prospective players or their parents by a staff member or authorized representative of an OBA-HP Program for the purpose of securing a prospective player’s position at a try-out or on a team operated by the HP-OBA Program. Releases and Playing Rights: Players who wish to sign with an HP-OBA Program for the following season: do not require a release under Section RP1.5 if included on an HP-OBA Program roster submitted by the applicable HP-OBA Roster Deadline; must obtain all releases required by Section RP1.5 if signed after the applicable HP-OBA Roster Deadline. If denied, an appeal may be filed in accordance with Section RP1.6; have their playing rights for non-High Performance Program play remain with the Local Association(s) and Affiliated Association determined in accordance with Section RP1.5. Classification and Competition: HP-OBA Programs: shall be classified AAA; may compete in any league they wish. HP INDEPENDENT PROGRAMS (HP-INDEPENDENT PROGRAM) Registration To register as an HP-Independent Program, the program must: not be part of a Local Association or Affiliated Association; annually declare to be an HP-Independent Program and pay the prescribed fees as determined by the OBA Board of Directors; not register any player 11U or younger on any team operated by such HP-Independent Program during the playing season. Opportunities: HP-Independent Program have players drafted to the team(s) representing Ontario at a Baseball Canada National Championship; have players on the Ontario Youth Team; have access to the OBA Pitch Count app and OBA OnDeck app at no cost; have access to any level of OBA certified umpires; choose to purchase OBA insurance. HP-Independent Programs may not compete in any Rep Play-off or Rep National Qualifier Tournament. An HP-Independent Program may participate in an OBA-sanctioned tournament at the discretion of the host. A player with an HP-Independent Program may not roster with an OBA Rep team other than in accordance with RP1.12 g). [TOP] RP1.14 High Performance Provincial Championship A High Performance Provincial Championship may be created in the future provided that the details of such a High Performance Provincial Championship are brought to the Rep Members for approval. Any such proposal would allow any HP-OBA Program or HP-Independent Program to participate if they meet requirements approved by the Rep Members (and would be operated in such a way as to not prevent HP-OBA Programs from also competing in the Rep Play-offs for their age category). A proposal shall address requirements for rostering, insurance, and arm care in order to participate. [TOP] RP1.15 Elite Baseball League of Ontario (EBLO) The OBA has established the Elite Baseball League of Ontario (EBLO) so that OBA players may compete at the highest level at their respective age levels. The vision for the EBLO is to offer as competitive a Rep league as possible. All OBA Rep rules and procedural requirements will be followed except as outlined below: Participating Affiliated Associations Any Affiliated Association desiring to participate in the EBLO is required to advise the Baseball Ontario Office and the EBLO Chair of its intent by July 1st. Such Affiliated Association shall continue to be a Participating Affiliated Association until it files a notice under clause RP1.15 (i)(b). Any Affiliated Association that wishes to withdraw from the EBLO is required to advise the Baseball Ontario Office and the EBLO Chair by July 1st. EBLO Structure The Rep Commissioner, subject to Rep Council approval, will appoint an EBLO Chair. The EBLO Chair shall oversee EBLO operations. The EBLO Steering Committee shall be comprised of the EBLO Chair plus other 5 members. Every 3 years, the number of teams entered in the EBLO by the Participating Affiliated Associations will be reviewed and the 4 with the most teams having been entered will each be entitled to one member on the EBLO Steering Committee. These Participating Affiliated Associations shall provide the name of their representative to the Baseball Ontario Office by October 25th of each year. As of November 2020, the 4 are currently TBA, COBA, EOBA and YSBA. The next review will take place for November 2023. The 5th spot will be elected by the Participating Affiliated Associations and Participating Associations. Nominations for this spot are due to the Baseball Ontario Office by October 25th of each year. Should one of the 4 Participating Affiliated Associations entitled to a member not identify their representative by October 25th, then that spot shall be declared vacant and shall be filled by election from among those nominated for the 5th spot on the EBLO Steering Committee. The EBLO Steering Committee shall be entitled to fill a vacancy on the EBLO Steering Committee arising during the year. Each member of the EBLO Steering Committee shall have one vote; provided, however, that the EBLO Chair shall only vote in the event of a tie. Except as reserved to the Participating Associations in this section RP1.14, the EBLO Steering Committee shall be authorized to make decisions in the best interests of the EBLO and its Participating Associations and consistent with the vision for the EBLO and the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules and the Rep Division Procedures and Playing Rules. Participating Affiliated Associations, which participated in the EBLO the previous season, shall be entitled to one vote. A Participating Affiliated Association, which did not participate in the EBLO the previous season, shall not be entitled to a vote. Participating Associations, which participated in the EBLO the previous season, shall be entitled to one-third of a vote for each Elite team that the Participating Association has entered into the EBLO during the previous three seasons. The voting total for the Participating Association shall be rounded to the nearest whole number of votes. A Participating Association, which did not participate in the EBLO the previous season, shall not be entitle to any votes. The elected member(s) of the Steering Committee shall be elected at an EBLO meeting to be held during the last week of November each year. Entering Teams Any Local Association located within a Participating Affiliated Association or a Participating Affiliated Association on its own behalf (a “Participating Association”) may enter Elite teams at the 14U, 15U, 16U, and 18U age level. An Affiliated Association is entering a team on its own behalf if it has created and is operating the team itself at the Affiliated Association level and is not merely approving a roster of a Local Association. The Participating Association must register the Elite teams to be entered with the Baseball Ontario Office and the EBLO Chair by July 1st for the following playing year. The registration of Elite team(s) by a Local Association requires the consent of the applicable Affiliated Association. If the Affiliated Association rejects the registration of its Local Association’s Elite team(s), the Local Association may appeal to the EBLO Steering Committee. In order for a Participating Association to register an Elite team at 16U, the Participating Association must have an Elite team at either (i) 14U or 15U or (ii) 18U or both. In order for a Participating Association to register an Elite team at 18U, the Participating Association must have an Elite team at either (i) 14U or 15U or (ii) 16U or both. A Participating Association may register an Elite team at 14U or 15U without having an Elite team at either 16U or 18U. The EBLO Steering Committee is permitted to review and rule on requests for exceptions to the program policy as may be requested by a Local Association. Each Elite team shall post with Baseball Ontario a performance bond in an amount determined by the EBLO Steering Committee from time to time. The performance bond may be called upon should the Elite team fail to comply with its obligations under the EBLO’s benchmarks, guidelines and playing rules. Tryouts and Releases There will be 14U, 15U, 16U and 18U teams, with 14U, 15U, 16U and 18U aged players, participating in the EBLO. Tryouts for these Elite teams, which are not HP-OBA Program teams, may start as early as July 1st and must be completed no later than the second Sunday in September. Try-outs for Elite teams which are HP-OBA Program teams are governed by the provisions of Section RP1.13. The respective Elite teams will determine the number of players on their team within the guidelines as outlined in the Procedures and Playing Rules of the Rep Division. It is recommended that Elite teams have at least 14 players on their roster. Except as set forth in clauses RP1.15 iv) e) and f), any player signed by an Elite team, which is not an HP-OBA Program team, will be deemed to have a Local Association, and where required an Affiliated Association, release as long as the applicable Affiliated Association has agreed to participate in the EBLO. Releases for the Elite teams which are HP-OBA Program teams are governed by the provisions of Section RP1.13. A 15U team, which is not an HP-OBA Program team, which proposes to sign more than 2 14U-aged players to its roster, and all players intending sign with such team are not entitled to rely upon RP1.15 iv) d) and will require a Local Association and, where required, an Affiliated Association release. To be clear a 15U team, which is not an HP-OBA Program team, may have more than 2 14U-aged players if it complies with this clause RP1.15 e). A player requesting to be signed by an Elite team, which is not an HP-OBA Program team, after the second Sunday in September will require a Local Association and, where required, an Affiliated Association release. If the requested release is denied, the Rep release appeal process set out in RP1.6 is available. It is understood that the playing rights of a player, who tries out for one or more Elite team(s) and is not selected, will remain where they were as determined by section RP1.5 b) of the Procedures and Playing Rules of the Rep Division. Each Elite team, which is not an HP-OBA Program team, will forward their roster to the Baseball Ontario Office no later than the Wednesday following the second Sunday in September. The Elite team will copy its Affiliated Association on the submission of its roster to the Baseball Ontario Office. Roster dates for Elite teams which are HP-OBA Program teams are governed by Section RP1.13. All managers, coaches and assistant coaches must have obtained the coaching certificate level to be eligible to participate at the Rep National Qualifiers for the age category. In the case of 14U Elite teams, the 15U Rep National Qualifiers shall be the applicable requirement. In the case of 16U Elite teams, the 18U Rep National Qualifiers shall be the applicable requirement. Any players, Head Coaches, Assistant Coaches or managers that participate in non-recognized programs are not eligible to participate in the EBLO. League Play There must be a minimum of six teams at a series to form an EBLO League for the respective age group. The EBLO shall schedule a meeting of Participating Affiliated Associations and Participating Associations in the spring to establish benchmarks, guidelines and playing rules for the subsequent playing season (e.g., a meeting in the spring of 2021 would establish benchmarks, guidelines and playing rules for the 2022 season). At such meeting, each Participating Affiliated Association and Participating Association shall have the votes set out in clauses RP1.15 ii) d) and e). The benchmarks, guidelines and playing rules established must be consistent with the vision for the EBLO and the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules and the Rep Division Procedures and Playing Rules. If the EBLO desires to enact a benchmark, guideline or playing rule inconsistent with the Rep Division Procedures and Playing Rules, then the EBLO requires the approval of Rep Council to enact such provision. In June of each year, the EBLO shall host an information meeting to present the benchmarks, guidelines and playing rules established at the spring meeting to prospective Elite teams interested in participating in the EBLO in the subsequent season. EBLO shall charge Elite teams a marketing fee established by Baseball Ontario’s Board of Management and Rep Council in consultation with the EBLO Steering Committee. All Elite teams will be classified as AAA. Notwithstanding clause RP3 k), at least one Level III umpire shall be assigned to each EBLO game. (The preference is that all umpires assigned to EBLO games be at least Level III.) If the home team is unable to arrange for the assignment of at least one Level III umpire, then the Head Coach of the home team must notify the Head Coach of the visiting team of the situation at least 48 hours in advance of the scheduled start of the game. The Head Coach of the visiting team shall have the option of either playing the game or postponing the game. [TOP] RP2 - Special Playing Rules The Official Rules of Baseball shall govern all games except as provided in the playing rules below. NOTE: Where there is a change made to The Official Rules of Baseball, the change shall not take effect until the Rep Council approves the application of the change. RP2.1 Uniforms & Equipment In all games in all Rep series, all team personnel, including the Head Coach, Assistant Coaches and manager, must be in complete baseball uniform. Note: A Complete baseball uniform shall consist of: corresponding tops or jerseys (with proper numbers) corresponding (similar) baseball caps baseball pants (no shorts) corresponding stirrup or baseball socks Provided the teams are completely uniformed the uniformity as laid down by the Official Playing Rules of Baseball will be disregarded and no protest will be considered by the OBA after the game has been played. The on-deck batter, batter, base runners, and batboy/batgirl must wear a prescribed protective helmet with flaps, covering both ears. Chinstraps and C-Flaps may be worn at the players' discretion but are not required at any age category. Senior aged players shall be permitted to wear either single ear or double ear flapped helmets in Senior OBA play. In T‐Ball, 8U, 9U, 10U, 11U, 12U, and 13U and 16U Girls shoes with metal cleats are prohibited. Beginning 2026: at 13U and below, shows with metal cleats are prohibited. All catchers are required to wear a mask with a throat protector, a helmet, chest protector, shin guards, and protective cup. Any team member serving as a catcher to warm up a pitcher shall wear a mask with a throat protector. Penalty: Coaches and players will be instructed to wear a mask and warned on first offense for failure to comply with this rule. Only one warning per team will be given throughout the game. After warning, any further violation of this rule by any team member will result in their immediate ejection. A team member shall be deemed as "warming up a pitcher" the moment either: The pitcher stands on the rubber (on the field or in the bullpen) the team member assumes a squatting position to receive a thrown ball from the pitcher, regardless of their position on the field or bullpen; or The team member assumes a position (whether standing, kneeling, or squatting) behind home plate. Players, Head Coaches, Assistant Coaches or managers who use electronic devices on the field will be ordered by the umpire to remove them from the field immediately. Failure to do so will result in ejection. Umpires who use electronic devices on the field during the game are subject to suspension and potential loss of accreditation. Fitbits, FuelBands, and other fitness trackers are considered electronic devices for the purposes of this rule and are not permitted except where required for medical reasons (i.e.: heart rate monitor prescribed by a physician). Exception: Dedicated watches, stopwatches and electronic pitch counters/indicators are permitted. Commercial electronic pitch communication systems (ie: PitchCom) are permitted at all Rep play. They are not considered electronic devices for the purposes of RP2.5(h). All players, including pitchers, are permitted to wear compression sleeves on one or both arms. If only wearing one, it may be worn on either the pitching or glove arm. A compression sleeve extending only to the elbow shall have the same restrictions as a compression sleeve extending to the wrist. Pitchers must wear compression sleeves which are solid in colour and may not be white or or grey. Pitchers may not wear compression sleeves which are distracting in nature including designs and reflective material. Except the pitcher, players may wear a playbook arm band on their arm or attache to their belt. Pitchers must remove these bands prior to taking the mound. With the exception of piping (strings), no part of the pitcher’s glove may be white or grey. The pitcher’s glove may not, in the judgement of the umpire, be distracting in any manner. No fielder, regardless of position may use a fielding glove that, in the judgement of the umpire, mimics, or attempts to mimic the colour of a baseball. [TOP] RP2.2 Game Length All games in a Rep Play‐off or National Qualifier Tournament shall be 7 complete innings (6 1/2 complete innings if the home team is ahead), or until a winner is declared. A 7-inning game will be considered an official game after 4 innings of play or 3-1/2 innings if the the home team is ahead. Exception: Rep Provincial Championship or National Qualifier Tournaments must be played to completion; see RP2.2(a). In tournament play if a game is delayed due to curfew, darkness, light failure, or weather-related conditions at any point prior to completion, the game shall be resumed from the point of delay. In the case of bad weather or other emergency situations, the schedule may be revised at the discretion of the Tournament Committee. In home and home style Rep Play‐off games called due to curfew, darkness, light failure, or weather-related conditions after becoming an official game shall be declared a complete and official game even if less than 7 innings were If the game is called prior to becoming an official game, then it is resumed from the point of delay. Except at 10U/11U, time limits are not permitted in any Rep Play‐off or National Qualifier games. In Rep Open or Invitational Tournaments when a time limit or curfew is in effect in the tournament rules, it shall be interpreted as follows: No new inning may be started after the time limit or curfew. An inning shall be deemed to have started immediately after the last out in the bottom of the previous inning has occurred. In all OBA Rep games, the following mercy rule will apply: 18 runs after 3 innings 15 runs after 4 innings 10 runs after 5 innings or any subsequent inning thereafter. NOTE: If the home team leads after the top half of any such inning, the game shall be considered a mercy and the home team will not bat. Where the home team scores to take the lead in the 7th inning, any extra inning or any inning that will be the last inning because of a time limit, the game ends immediately. Similarly, if the home team scores a run that results in a mercy pursuant to g), the game ends immediately with that run. [TOP] RP2.3 Line-Up Rules Each Head Coach will see that the umpire‐in‐chief is provided with a batting order card listing the batting order of his/her players for the commencement of the game. This card shall list (i.e., print legibly) each player’s first and last name, their uniform number and position. It shall also list the substitute players, Head Coaches, Assitant coaches and manager including their uniform number. Bona fide players of either team, who arrive after the commencement of the game, may have their names added to the list upon reporting to the umpire‐in‐chief. In 8U, 9U, 10U and 11U games, all players present at the game shall be listed on the team’s batting line‐up in the order desired by the Head Coach and bat in that late arrivals shall be added to the bottom of this batting order. In 10U and 11U games, if a player is removed from the game due to injury, illness or official ejection, the player’s spot in the batting order shall be deleted. In the event a batter must be removed due to injury, official ejection, or has to leave for any reason during the time at bat, the next batter in the batting order shall come to bat assuming the balls and strikes count at the time the batter is removed. If the batter becomes an out, such batter shall then take their regular turn at bat. Extra Player Rule: In 12U, 13U, 14U and 15U, teams may opt to utilize 10 batters in the starting line‐up. The additional batter shall be deemed as the Extra Player (EP). The following rules for the EP shall apply: The option to use the EP must be declared when handing in the line‐up card prior to the start of the game. The EP may bat in any place in the batting order. The EP will be considered a defensive position and eligible for re‐entry. If a team using the EP has any players ejected or injured and it results in the team having less than 10 players in the line‐up the game shall continue with 9 players. The batting order will be shifted up to fill the void but otherwise remains the same. Note: If the player starting in the EP is moved into a defensive position his place in the batting order does not change. The new EP will bat in the place of the player they replaced. At 16U and 18U, a team may use either an EP or a designated hitter, but the team may not use both an EP and a designated hitter in the same game. For clarity, a team may also choose not to use either an EP or a designated hitter. A designated hitter may be used for a pitcher from 16U to Senior as per the Official Rules of Baseball. For tournament play the Tournament Convenor shall appoint an official scorer for each game (who may be the scorer of one of the teams playing). For all home and home style OBA Play‐off games the home team score keeper shall be the official score keeper. Senior AAA teams are permitted to mutually affiliate themselves with any one 22U team within their Affiliated Association as long as their Local Association does not have a 22U team. If the Baseball Canada 22U Nationals are hosted by an Ontario team – the players on the 22U host team are only permitted to be called up to a Senior team during National Qualifiers as long as they are a member of the same local association. Senior AAA teams are permitted a maximum of 5 22U‐aged call‐ups for National Qualifiers. At 16U and above, in the event the starting pitcher will bat for themselves as the DH, the player will be considered two separate people for the purposes of OBR 5.11(a). In such cases, the Head Coach should list 10 players on the line-up card and this player should be listed twice – once as the starting pitcher and once as the Designated Hitter. Thus, if the starting pitcher is replaced, they can continue as the DH (but can no longer pitch in the game), and if the DH is replaced, they can continue as the pitcher (but can no longer hit for themselves). If the player is simultaneously replaced both as the starting pitcher and the DH, they cannot be replaced by another two-way player filling both roles; they must be replaced by two separate players. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in OBR 5.11(a), if the starting pitcher/DH is switched to a position on defense instead of DH, such move will terminate the DH role for the remainder of the game. At Senior AAA, all teams are required to use Game Changer to score all regular season, tournament and National Qualifier Tournament games. [TOP] RP2.4 Substitutions Except as required in RP2.10(c), in 10U and 11U games, unlimited substitutions defensively are allowed (including for injury or official ejection). From 12U to 18U and Senior Women, any starting player may be substituted for and returned to the game one time only. They must return to their original place in the batting order. Any further substitutions do not jeopardize the right of re‐entry for the original/starting player. Any starting player returned to the game may pitch as long as they have not previously assumed the pitching position. A pinch runner or pinch hitter may be inserted for a pitcher, who was listed in the starting batting order, and the pitcher may return to pitch under this re‐entry rule only if the player is re‐entered immediately as a pitcher when his/her team takes the field defensively. At all levels, courtesy runners for the catcher or any other players are prohibited. Pinch runners may be substituted in accordance with the Official Rules of Baseball except where contravened herein. From 12U to 18U and Senior Women, in the event that all players have entered the game and an injury(s) occurs, the opposing Head Coach shall have the right of selecting any remaining player(s) as the replacement. Note: This rule does not apply to ejections from a game. If a player re‐enters or enters the game illegally: offensively, this shall be considered as a batter hitting out of order; defensively, the proper player shall replace them. [TOP] RP2.5 Pitching Rules See also: Arm Care Rules in the OBA Playing Rules. In 10U and 11U, the number of trips to the mound by the Head Coach shall be limited to 3 trips per inning except when, in the judgement of the umpire, the purpose of the trip is to delay the game. In 12U and 13U, a third trip by the Head Coach to the same pitcher in the same inning shall result in the pitcher’s automatic removal from the pitching position. Except at Senior, a pitcher once removed from the pitching position, regardless of the number of trips to the mound by the Head Coach, may continue in the game at another position but shall not be permitted to return to pitch in that game. Exception: Where re-entry rules apply, pitchers who are substituted for offensively by either a pinch runner or pinch hitter may return to pitch in the next defensive inning so long as no other player has pitched for that team. From 14U to Senior, a second trip by a Head Coach to the same pitcher in the same inning shall result in the pitcher’s automatic removal from the pitching position. The pitcher, once removed after the second trip in an inning may continue to play at another position but shall not be permitted to return to pitch in that game. When a pitcher takes their position at the beginning of each inning, or when they relieve another pitcher, they shall be permitted to pitch up to eight preparatory pitches to the catcher, during which play shall be suspended. The plate umpire may reduce this number if, in their judgement, there has been unnecessary delay by the pitcher and/or catcher. If a sudden emergency causes a pitcher to be summoned into the game without sufficient opportunity to warm up, the plate umpire shall allow them as many pitches as the plate umpire deems necessary. [TOP] RP2.6 Base Running Rules Malicious Contact: Malicious contact is to be penalized whether committed by an offensive or defensive player. Contact shall be considered malicious if: the contact is the result of intentional excessive force, and/or there is an intent to injure. The umpire shall determine whether contact was avoidable or if the fielder blocks the base path, the runner may make contact, slide into or collide with the fielder as long as the runner is making a legitimate attempt to reach the base/plate. Runners are instructed to slide or attempt to avoid making contact with a fielder. A player who maliciously runs into another player is to be declared out (unless he/she has scored prior to committing the infraction) and is to be automatically ejected (whether or not declared out). If the defensive player blocked the base path clearly without possession of the ball, obstruction shall be called. The runner is safe, and a delayed dead ball is called. [TOP] RP2.7 Fielding Rules A fielder may not enter the dugout or bench area to make a legal catch. Any such ball shall be declared dead. Where a fielder carries a live ball into dead ball area, the ball shall be declared dead, and all runners shall be awarded 1 base without liability of being put out. [TOP] RP2.8 EXTRA-INNING RULES At 22U and below, the following extra inning procedure will be followed when the game is tied at the end of regulation play for all Baseball Ontario Rep sanctioned tournaments, Rep Provincial Championships, and Rep National Qualifier Tournaments: Each team will begin the extra inning (and any subsequent necessary extra innings) with a player on second base only, no outs. The batter with the last full plate appearance in the previous inning will be placed at second base. The traditional system of the visiting team hitting in the top of the inning and the home team hitting in the bottom of the inning will remain in effect until a winner is determined. All other Official Baseball Rules and OBA rules remain in effect during extra innings. Example: If the last inning ends with the #6 batter having the last plate appearance, then the extra inning begins with the #7 batter at bat, and the #6 batter at second base. If however, the #6 batter was still at-bat when the inning ended, that batter would remain at bat in the extra inning and the #5 batter would be placed at second base to start the extra inning. [TOP] RP2.9 Protests Protests based on umpires’ judgment decisions will not be permitted. A protest based on an umpire’s interpretation of the rules must be lodged with the umpire before the game resumes, and the opposing team must also be advised by the umpire before the game resumes. No protests will be entertained regarding coaches or managers being listed on a team roster. A coach or manager signed with one team in an organization may act as coach or manager for any other team in that organization. Tournament Style Rep Provincial Championships: A Protest Committee shall be established by the host Local Association. The Protest Committee has the right to rule whether a protest will be heard. All protests must be ruled on at the time of their occurrence by the Protest Committee. If the protest is heard and denied, the team will be billed $50. Home and Home Style Rep Provincial Championships: Protests are to be emailed to the Rep Protest Chair postmarked within 24 hours of the end of the game under protest. A protest fee of $50.00 must be electronically transferred. The Protest Chair will obtain a copy of the Umpire's report regarding the incident. The Protest Chair will obtain written reports from each Head Coach regarding the incident. The Protest Chair, in consultation with the Rep Commissioner, will determine if there is reason for holding a protest meeting. Should a protest meeting be deemed necessary, the Panel shall include the Series Chair, the Rep Commissioner, and the Protest Chair (Competition Chair). Results of the Protest Meeting will be delivered by the Protest Chair to the Rep Commissioner, both teams, the OBA Office, the Rep Series Chair, and the Rep Overall Series Chair. Once a protest has been dealt with by the OBA or a committee delegated to act in such matters by the OBA, any ruling handed down must be considered as final by the OBA. No further action will be taken. The Rep Protest Chair has the power in a home and home style OBA Play‐off to appoint a committee to handle all protests which must be disposed of 5 days from the time the Rep Protest Chair receives the complete protest, etc. All correspondence regarding protests and defence of appeals must be forwarded by email. Even if it is held that the protested decision violated the rules, no replay of the game will be ordered unless in the opinion of the Rep Protest Chair the violation adversely affected the protesting team’s chances of winning the game. Teams or players which knowingly play against ineligible players and do wilfully conceal this knowledge from the OBA until it shall suit their convenience to use it in any protest which may arise shall be considered equally guilty with the offending team or players and shall be dealt with accordingly. Emergency Situations: In an emergency situation the protest will be dealt with by the Rep Series Chair in consultation with the Rep Protest Chair and the Rep Commissioner. [TOP] RP2.10 10U/11U Playing Rules Home base shall be marked by a five‐sided slab of rubber. It shall be a 21‐inch square with two of the corners removed so that the front edge is 21‐inches wide, and the back of the plate comes to a point at the intersection of the lines extending from home base to first base and to third base. A team’s turn at bat in an inning will end after three outs have been recorded or four runs have been scored. The last inning will have an eight-run maximum. On any play where the last allowed run in an inning is as a result of the ball leaving the playing field and the awarding of home base (ie: home run, overthrow, etc) results in more than the maximum runs allowed in an inning scoring, all awarded runs will score; this only applies to dead ball awards. Ex. 1: Runners on 1st, 2nd and 3rd. 2 outs. 3 runs have already scored in the inning. The batter hits a groundball to the shortstop, who overthrows first base. The ball goes out of play. Because this is the first play by an infielder, all runners get awarded 2 bases from the time of pitch. 2 runs score for a total of 5 for the inning. Ex. 2: Runners on 2nd and 3rd. 2 outs. 3 runs have already scored in the inning. The batter hits a ball in the gap between outfielders. Despite the fact that two runs would have scored easily, because the ball stayed in play, the inning ends when the runner from 3rd touches the plate. 1 run scores on the play for a total of 4 runs for the inning. Ex. 3: Runners on 2nd and 3rd. 3 runs have already scored in the inning. The batter hits a ball down the left field line which rolls past an out of play line. Ball is dead. This is a 2-base award, so 2 runs score on this play, with a total of 5 for the inning. The following mercy rules shall apply at 10U/11U only: 18 runs after 3 innings 15 runs after 4 innings 10 runs after 5 innings 9 runs after 6 innings. If the home team leads by mercy after the top half of any such inning, the game shall be considered a mercy and the home team will not bat. No new inning shall be permitted to start after 2 hours have elapsed from the start of the game. No time limit during the final championship game. Local tournament rules for regular season tournaments can impose a shorter time limit. In all Rep Play‐off tournament games and all play‐off round games of regular season tournaments must have a winner declared, so extra innings are to be played regardless of the time limit. A 10U/11U game ends when the time limit is reached and a full inning has been completed. If the time limit passes during an inning, such inning will be the last inning however it will not be treated as the last inning for the purposes of the 8-run maximum rule. If the top of a new inning is started within 15 minutes of the time limit, the umpire will declare that inning to be the last inning and the 8-run maximum will apply. A 10U/11U game ends at any time when a team is unable to score enough runs to win or tie the game based on the innings remaining in the game and the prescribed run limits per inning (RP2.10 (b), excluding runs allowed because of dead ball awards. In games where extra innings are required to determine a winner, the 8 run maximum will apply for all extra innings. Unlimited substitutions defensively are allowed (including for injury or official ejection). The number of trips to the mound by the Coach shall be limited to 3 trips per inning per pitcher. Base runners cannot leave the base until the ball leaves the pitcher’s hand. If an infraction occurs, the ball will be declared dead, and runners may not advance. One warning will be issued and for any further infractions thereafter, the runner will be called out. (Each team to receive 1 warning). If a player is removed from the game due to injury, illness, or official ejection, the player’s spot in the batting order shall be deleted. In the event a batter must be removed during their time at bat, the next batter in the batting order shall come to bat assuming the balls and strikes count at the time the batter is removed. If the batter becomes an out, such batter shall then take their regular turn at bat. If a runner is removed by reason of injury or official ejection from the game, the player last put out shall act as pinch runner for the removed player. By virtue of the rules there shall be no pinch runners allowed. The batter shall not become a runner when a third strike is not legally caught by the catcher. The ball remains live, and all other runners may advance at their own risk. [TOP] RP5 REP Provincial Championships The rules in RP5 apply only to Rep Provincial Championships (OBA Championships, Qualifiers, and Playdowns) RP5.1 Rep Provincial Championship Procedures The OBA shall conduct and assist the different Affiliated Association representatives to meet and play off to determine the OBA champions. The Rep Council shall annually set the fees for the OBA Rep Provincial Championships. Once Classifications have been finalized, failure to participate in the Rep Provincial Championships shall result in a fine equivalent to 1.5 times the amount of the Provincial CHampionship fee as determined by Rep Council annually. The Rep Council shall appoint a Series Chair to handle each series Rep Provincial Championships. In order to be eligible to participate in the Rep Provincial Championships, teams must represent a city, town or municipality within the boundaries of the Province of Ontario. Players must be bona fide and continuous residents of the province in accordance with the Rep Rules and Regulations. Whenever possible, the Rep Provincial Championships shall be a tournament. If no host is found by the July meeting of the Rep Council, then that series shall become a home and home style Rep Provincial Championship. Rep Provincial Championship Tournaments will be comprised of 1 team from each Affiliated Association, which has classified a team for such Rep Provincial Championship Tournament, plus a host team. At 22U and Senior, the AAA Provincial Championship shall be the Rep National Qualifier Tournament. Coaches must have a copy of their OBA approved roster with them in case it is required during the tournament. Barring extenuating circumstances, if 1 team is not able to start a game within 30 minutes of the scheduled starting time for such game, the game will be forfeited to the other team and in tournament style Rep Provincial Championships, the offending team will not be allowed to continue in the tournament. The game will count as a game played and not count as a bye in the tournament and the published chart shall not be changed. Any team failing to play a game when ordered will automatically forfeit their right to continue in Rep Provincial Championships. This causes a $500.00 fine to be assessed to the team forfeiting. A team that has been suspended shall have the right to appeal to the Rep Council for a hearing. All appeals must be accompanied with a fee of $50.00. The Rep Tournament Chair shall provide, immediately, to the Affiliated Associations a listing of any and all disciplinary problems with Head Coaches, Assistant Coaches, managers, players or other team personnel, as reported by the Series Chair. In all Rep Provincial Championship games, the diamond must be properly laid out; the batter’s box marked; the pitcher’s mound properly built up and the playing field kept sufficiently clear of spectators and obstructions for the proper performance of the players. [TOP] RP5.2 Tournament Style Rep Provincial Championships When the number of entrants to a tournament-style Rep Provincial Championship is restricted by other organizations, such as those tournaments played during the Ontario Summer Games, a preliminary round(s) shall be arranged to provide the necessary representatives.
