title: Select Division Procedures
snippet: Skip to main content Select Division Procedures – Select Rule Book COACHING CENTRE UMPIRE ZONE FEMALE BASEBALL ARM CARE MORE SIGN IN / REGISTER Select Division Procedures Updated for the 2026 Baseball Season Table of Contents S1.01 General S1.02 Select Divisio
searchText: Select Division Procedures Select Division Procedures Skip to main content Select Division Procedures – Select Rule Book COACHING CENTRE UMPIRE ZONE FEMALE BASEBALL ARM CARE MORE SIGN IN / REGISTER Select Division Procedures Updated for the 2026 Baseball Season Table of Contents S1.01 General S1.02 Select Division S1.03 Select Membership and Eligibility S1.04 Select Council Structure S1.04.1 Select Council S1.04.2 Select Terms of Office and Elections S1.04.3 Select Voting S1.04.4 Select Life Members S1.05 Duties of Councillors S1.06 Meetings S1.06.1 Select Annual Meeting S1.06.2 Select Council Meetings S1.06.3 Select Special Division Meetings S1.07 Amendments S1.08 Rule Changes S1.09 Select Provincial Championships S1.01 GENERAL In addition to the provisions of the OBA By-Laws and the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules, select play shall be governed by the provisions of the Procedures and Playing Rules of the Select Division, which may not include any provision that conflicts with the OBA By-Laws or the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules. To the extent that there is any conflict between the OBA By-Laws, the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules and/or the Select Procedures and Playing Rules, the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules shall prevail. The Select Procedures and Playing Rules as shown in this document are intended as a general guide and cannot specifically cover each situation that may arise. Therefore, the Commissioner in regular or special meetings, with a quorum of the Council, will have the authority to interpret and decide to the best of their judgement with regard to all the circumstances or any specific case, any matter pertaining to any and all clauses contained herein. Their decision shall be final. The Select Division acknowledges that the OBA Board of Directors is empowered to impose rule changes in the best interests of baseball as per paragraph A1.01(e) of the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules. Any Select member association and/or officers of same, which fails to uphold the rulings and enforce the decisions of the OBA will leave said Select Member Association, and/or officers open to suspension by the OBA. [TOP] S1.02 Select Division The Select Division shall work: to enhance the quality of select baseball for the youth of Ontario while stressing the values of sportsmanship, athleticism, teamwork, and fair play; and To foster the “select” philosophy of play, where all eligible team players at a game must play in the game unless removed due to injury or ejection. to provide house league baseball tournaments for the youth of Ontario. The Select Division shall regulate competition throughout the province at the select level, including provincial championships. Select baseball is defined as the game of baseball played by the amended baseball rules as approved by the Select Council for all sanctioned select tournaments and championships. A select player shall be defined as: playing regularly on a dedicated select team playing in a loop; and/or playing regularly on a dedicated house league team playing in a local community house league; registered as a player by the local community baseball association; agreeing to abide by the Select Procedures and Playing Rules of sanctioned select events. The following players are ineligible to play select: Rep Elite PBLO from Programs not recognized by Baseball Ontario No player may be disqualified from playing on a Select Division team for reason of race, religion, gender, or sexual orientation. [TOP] S1.03 Select Membership and Eligibility Membership shall be open to all local community baseball associations within the province, which meet all the criteria as specified in the Select Division Procedures and Playing Rules, and any applicable Select Division Policies. A club or organization shall be defined as a structured group which provides a recognized baseball program within the guidelines of this Select Division Procedures and Playing Rules. Each request for membership shall be reviewed and approved by the Select Council on an individual basis to ensure that each club and/or association understands the requirements of the Select Division, its Select Playing Rules and the mandate of the Select Council. Members may also be members of other organizations or associations if they do not conflict with the mission and objectives of the Select Division. Managers, coaches, Select Council members, and Select Member Association board members shall immediately disqualify themselves for participation within Baseball Ontario's Select Division and its Select Member Associations, if they are directly involved in any way with non-recognized programs within the Province of Ontario as defined in the Ontario Baseball Association Bylaws. Members shall be in good standing with: all fees paid in full by June 30th of the current playing season; all fines paid prior to the Select AGM. All team player rosters of member associations in good standing must be submitted by June 30th, failure to comply will result in a fine of $50.00 per team. For all membership definitions, please refer to the Select Membership Policy. Member community baseball associations shall designate a delegate and an alternate who will represent them at meetings of members. Names of the delegate and alternate shall be submitted to the OBA Office. They must be received on the letterhead of the local community association and shall be in effect until an alternate appointment or resignation is received, from the club or association, by the Select Division. Both the delegate and the alternate must be 18 years of age or older as of January 1 of the current year. [TOP] S1.04 SELECT COUNCIL STRUCTURE S1.04.1 Select Council The affairs of the Select Division shall be managed by the Select Council. The Select Commissioner, Select Deputy Commissioner and ten (10) Select Councillors shall be elected to serve on the Select Council. The term of each newly elected council member will begin at the next duly called meeting of the Select Council following the election. The Select Council shall elect an Operational Committee to manage the day-to-day operations of the Select Division. The Operational Committee shall be appointed by the Select Commissioner from the Select Council Members. The Select Operational Committee shall consist of up to five (5) members and shall include the Select Commissioner, the Deputy Select Commissioner and up to three (3) other members of Select Council as determined by the Select Commissioner. The Select Council shall structure, appoint and mandate Committees and members of Committees as required. Additional Select Councillors may be appointed to the Select Council to serve on the Select Council as required up to a maximum of fifteen (15) council members. A local community baseball association (member) can only have one (1) elected representative to the Select Council at any time. The President and Vice President of the OBA are deemed to be voting ex-officio members of Select Council. [TOP] S1.04.2 Select Terms of Office and Elections The Select Commissioner will be elected at the OBA Annual Meeting in accordance with the OBA By-laws. Nominations for Select Deputy Commissioner and Select Councillors shall be submitted annually to the OBA Office by a deadline established by the Board of Directors. Any Select Member in good standing may submit a nomination. All terms for elected Select Councillors shall be for two (2) years. These terms of office shall be staggered so that five (5) positions on the Select Council shall come open each year. Vacancies filled by appointment will serve out the elected incumbent’s term of office or until the next election whichever comes first. Operational Committee and other Committee appointments shall be for a term not to exceed one year from date of appointment or as specified by the Select Council. Any member of the Select Council who is absent for three (3) consecutive meetings, without reasons that in the opinion of the Select Council are satisfactory, shall have their voting rights forfeited. After two (2) consecutive absences the member in question will be notified. After three (3) consecutive absences their Select Council position may be rescinded subject to a vote by the Select Council. The Immediate Past Select Commissioner or designate of the Select Council will be responsible for determining a list of individuals indicating a willingness to stand for office. The Immediate Past Select Commissioner or designate will be responsible for conducting elections at the Select Annual Division Meeting with the assistance of individuals that are not standing for office. All nominees must be an accredited delegate or alternate of a member’s association and must either be present in person or have provided a written consent to stand to be filed by the nominator. No member shall hold more than one elected Operational position of the Select Council at any one time Elections for Select Council will be conducted by ballot. [TOP] S1.04.3 Select Voting All matters calling for a vote shall be determined by a majority of the voting members present and voting on the question. Voting procedures may be outlined at the meeting prior to the vote unless otherwise specified in this document. Each local Member and Loop Member, which is a member in good standing, shall have one vote at (i) Select Annual Division Meetings; (ii) Select Special Division Meetings; and (iii) Committee meetings to which they are duly appointed. Votes shall be cast by accredited Councillors or alternates at appropriate meetings only. Each member of the Select Council or a Committee has one vote apart from the chairperson of the Select Council or Committee who may cast a second vote in the event of a tie. [TOP] S1.04.4 Select Life Members The Select Council may grant Select Life Memberships. The criteria for granting a Select life membership is ten (10) years or more of faithful service by the individual and the desire on the part of the proposed Select Life Member to remain active in the affairs of the Select Council in an official capacity. The number of Select Life Members will have no limit. Select Life Members will be allocated no more than three (3) representatives on the Select Council. Select Honourary Member: The Select Council may name any of the past serving Select Council members as a Select Honourary Member in order that the individual may assist the Select Council in the convening of its Annual Select Division Provincial Tournaments. A maximum of three (3) Select Life Members positions will be included in the Select Council. Select Life Member Councillors will be duly nominated and voted on by those holding the Select Life Member designation. [TOP] S1.05 Duties of Councillors The Select Council shall be responsible for the overall direction of the Select Division The Select Council shall approve all policies of the Select Division. Select Council Past Presidents will have all the rights, privileges, and responsibilities of a select Past Commissioner. The duties and responsibilities of the Operational Committee are as follows: Select Immediate Past Commissioner The Select Commissioner shall serve as chairperson of the Operational Committee and the Select Council. Function as the chief operational officer for the Select Division. Ensure Meetings are called as specified. Ensure Meeting agendas are prepared. Carry out the policies and procedures as specified and outlined by the Select Council. Select Immediate Past Commissioner The Select Immediate Past Commissioner shall serve on the Operational Committee and the Select Council in an advisory capacity with voting privileges. Select Deputy Commissioner The Select Deputy Commissioner shall assist the Select Commissioner to carry out his/her duties. In the event that the Select Commissioner cannot carry out his/her duties for any reason, the Select Deputy Commissioner shall act on his/her behalf and execute the Select Commissioner’s responsibilities within the rules of this document. The Select Deputy Commissioner shall fulfill the duties outlined for the Select Deputy Commissioner in Select Division policies. Select Budget Chair Responsible for all financial affairs of the Select Division. Chair of the Financial Committee. Ensure the proper financial procedures are in place. Ensure that proper records are kept and circulated appropriately. Fulfill the duties outlined for the Select Budget Chair in Select Division policies. [TOP] S1.06 MEETINGS S1.06.1 Select Annual Meeting The Select Annual Division Meeting will be held in conjunction with the Rep Annual Division Meeting. Adequate notice shall be given to all Select Members of the time and place of the Select Annual Division Meeting (no less than 30 days prior to the meeting). A quorum shall be defined as fifteen (15) voting members present. Proxy votes are not allowed. The incumbent Select Commissioner or designate shall act as chairperson of the Select Annual Meeting, except with respect to elections which will be chaired by the Select Immediate Past Commissioner or designate. Unless otherwise provided in this Select Division Procedures and Playing Rules, all matters calling for a vote shall be determined by a majority show of hands of the voting members present except where a member requests voting to be held in another form. Approval of the alternate form of voting requires ratification by a majority of members and applies only to the specified motion. Proceedings shall be governed by Robert’s Rules of Order unless otherwise stated. The order of business of the Select Annual Division Meeting shall be as follows: Reports on the Select Division Amendments to the Select Procedures and Playing rules Announcements Election of Select Council [TOP] S1.06.2 Select Council Meetings Meetings of Select Council shall be held a minimum of three (3) times a year, separately or in conjunction with a Select Annual Division Meeting, Select Special Division Meeting, or a Select Business Meeting, to deal with matters of the Select Division. Notice of place and time of the first Select Council meeting shall be set at the Select Annual Division Meeting. Place and time of subsequent meetings shall be announced at each Select Council meeting. All matters calling for a vote shall be determined by a majority show of hands of the voting members of the Select Council. A quorum shall consist of 50% of the members of the Select Council. [TOP] S1.06.3 Select Special Division Meetings The Chair must call a Select Special Division Meeting when: Requested to do so by a majority of the members of the Select Council. Requested to do so in writing by 25% of the members of the Select Division. Matters or events, in his/her discretion, warrant the meeting. Adequate notice shall be given to all Select Members of the time and place of the Select Special Division Meeting (no less than 30 days prior to the meeting). [TOP] S1.07 Amendments Articles of the Select Division Procedures and Playing Rules may only be amended at the Select Annual Division Meeting, or a Select Special Division Meeting called for that purpose. Select Members desiring to amend the Select Division Procedures and Playing Rules shall submit such amendment(s) in writing to the OBA Office thirty-five (35) days in advance of the OBA Convention as announced by the OBA Board of Directors. Amendments must be approved by a majority of the votes cast by voting members present. At a Select Annual Meeting, the Select Council may propose a resolution for an experiment that differs from the Select Procedures and Playing Rules. This experiment may be put into effect as an experiment for no more than 2 years. In order to be adopted, the resolution must be approved by 2/3 of the votes cast in respect of the resolution and would be subject to annual review by the Select Council. Any references in the Select Division Procedures and Playing Rules that are contrary to the experiment are suspended while the experiment is in progress. Once the time period for the experiment has expired, unless an amendment to the Select Division Procedures and Playing Rules is adopted with respect to the subject matter of the experiment, the resolution shall be of no further force and effect and the suspension of any references in the Select Division Procedures and Playing Rules shall be removed. [TOP] S1.08 Rule Changes For the purposes of S1.08, "Playing Rules" shall include the Select Provincial Tournament Rules & Regulations and the Select Division Sanctioned Tournament Rules. All proposed changes to the Playing Rules must be submitted by: A member Local Association, or A member Loop Association, or A member of Select Council All proposed rule changes must be submitted tto the OBA Office no later than thirty-five (35) days in advance of the OBA Convention as announced by the OBA Board of Directors. The Baseball Ontario Office will distribute proposed rules changes to all Member Local Associations and Member Loop Associations within 7 days. [TOP] S1.09 Select Provincial Championships Depending on the number of entrants, the OBA shall offer for annual competition Select Provincial Championships at 9U, 11U, 13U, 15U, 18U, and 22U. At the discretion of Select Council, and depending on the number of entrants, Select Provincial Championships may be segregated into separate championships between Dedicated Select teams and House League Select teams. Each recognized Select Loop with teams at a particular age category shall be awarded a minimum of one entry into the Select Provincial Championship at such age category. In the discretion of the Select Council, Select Loops may be awarded multiple spots in the Select Provincial Championships. Where a Select team plays in a Select Loop, such team may not be awarded a Direct Entry into a Select Provincial Championship. Instead, the Select Loop in which they participate may be awarded multiple spots in the Select Provincial Championship. Select teams participating in a Select Loop through interlock must qualify for Select Provincial Championships through their own regional requirements or play-offs. Host teams and non-loop teams are not subject to S1.11(d) Select Provincial Championships shall be played in accordance with the Select Provincial Tournament Rules & Regulations published annually by Select Council. snippet: Skip to main content OBA Procedures and Playing Rules – OBA Procedures & Playing Rules COACHING CENTRE UMPIRE ZONE FEMALE BASEBALL ARM CARE MORE SIGN IN / REGISTER OBA Procedures and Playing Rules Updated for the 2026 Baseball Season Table of Contents A1.01 Ge
searchText: Oba Procedures And Playing Rules OBA Procedures and Playing Rules Skip to main content OBA Procedures and Playing Rules – OBA Procedures & Playing Rules COACHING CENTRE UMPIRE ZONE FEMALE BASEBALL ARM CARE MORE SIGN IN / REGISTER OBA Procedures and Playing Rules Updated for the 2026 Baseball Season Table of Contents A1.01 General A2.01 Budget Process A2.02 Setting Fees A2.03 Authority to Bind the Organization A2.04 Financial Claims A3.01 Insurance A4.01 Prohibition of Tobacco, Alcohol, and Drugs A5.01 Discipline A5.02 Penalties A5.03 Commissioner Appeals A5.04 President Appeals A5.05 Appeal of Fines A6.01 Reporting Abuse A7.01 Championship Awards A8.01 Coaches and Managers A9.01 Umpires - General A9.02 Umpire Fees A10.01 Double First Base (Safety Base) A11.01 Bat Rules A12.01 Approved Baseballs A13.01 Arm Care Rules A14.01 Female Baseball A15.01 Playing Non-Recognized Programs A16.01 Field Dimensions A17.01 Amendments A1.01 General The OBA Procedures and Playing Rules herein shall govern the Rep and Select Divisions of the organization, in addition to division-specific Procedures and Playing Rules as determined by the Divisions. To the extent that there is any conflict between these OBA Procedures and Playing Rules and/or the Select Procedures and Playing Rules and/or the Rep Procedures and Playing Rules, these OBA Procedures and Playing Rules shall prevail. The Official Rules of Baseball shall govern all games except as provided in the playing rules below. NOTE: Where there is a change made to The Official Rules of Baseball, the change shall not take effect until approved by OBA and/or its Councils as appropriate. The OBA Procedures and Playing Rules as shown in this document are intended as a general guide and cannot specifically cover each situation that may arise. Therefore, the President in regular or special meetings, with a quorum of the Board of Directors, will have the authority to interpret and decide to the best of their judgement regarding all the circumstances or any specific case, any matter pertaining to any and all clauses contained herein. Their decision shall be final. The Board of Directors may review and modify the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules, Rep Procedures and Playing Rules and/or Select Procedures and Playing Rules if the change(s) are determined to be in the best interests of baseball and/or matters that are related to safe sport (risk management). Such change(s) must be approved by a minimum of 75% of eligible votes. Any member association and/or officers of same, which fails to uphold the rulings and enforce the decisions of the OBA will leave said Member Association, and/or officers open to suspension by the OBA. [TOP] A2.01 Budget Process The Executive Committee shall prepare a budget annually for consideration and approval by OBA Board of Directors. OBA Board of Management shall have complete discretion over the establishment of fees for matters within the jurisdiction of the OBA Board of Management (eg. insurance, coaching clinics, umpire clinics, etc.). [TOP] A2.02 Setting Fees Rep Council has the authority to increase any fee applicable to the Rep Division and OBA Board of Directors shall include such increased fee in the OBA budget. Select Council has the authority to increase any fee applicable to the Select Division and OBA Board of Directors shall include such increased fee in the OBA budget. Where either Division or Council, or any subcommittee of same, including the Elite Division Steering Committee, desires to lower a fee, then such decrease is only effective upon the approval of the OBA Board of Directors. Where OBA Board of Directors desires to increase a fee applicable to either or both Divisions, such increase is only effective upon the approval of the Council(s). [TOP] A2.03 Authority to Bind the Organization Only the Board of Directors has the authority to enter into contracts that bind the organization. No member association or individual shall have any power or authority to bind the organization in any way, to pledge its credit or to render it liable pecuniarily for any purpose. No Member shall have any power or authority to bind the organization unless the Member has been authorized by the Board of Directors to act as agent of the organization in accordance with the previous sentence. [TOP] A2.04 Financial Claims Officers, members of the Board of Directors, members of Rep Council, members of Select Council, Series Chairs and Convenors appointed by the Board or Council(s) may be financially reimbursed for out‑of‑pocket expenses in attending OBA Board of Directors or committee meetings, and also for appropriate out of pocket expenses pertaining to OBA business during sanctioned events. All financial claims must be submitted to the OBA Office within 2 months of expenditure or by September 30th, whichever comes first. [TOP] A3.01 Insurance All teams of Local Associations, Affiliated Associations, Rep and/or Select Member Associations, Direct Entries and PBLO Franchises must have the liability insurance provided through the OBA. This insurance must be purchased before the teams, associations and franchises begin the new baseball season. For purposes of insurance coverage only, the OBA sanctions all usual baseball activities including games, practices, try-outs, evaluations, tournaments and executive activities conducted by its Affiliated Associations, Local Associations, Rep and/or Select Member Associations, Direct Entries and Associate Members and such other activities of the OBA, its Affiliated Associations, Local Associations, Rep and/or Select Member Associations, Direct Entries and Associate Members as may be approved from time to time by the OBA Board of Directors. [TOP] A4.01 Prohibition of Tobacco, Alcohol, and Drugs Players, coaches, and umpires in uniform must refrain from using any tobacco products during all games. Players, coaches, and umpires in uniform must not be under the influence of alcohol or drugs. Penalty for a) and b) will be immediate ejection for players or coaches and may result in loss of accreditation for umpires. [TOP] A5.01 Discipline Teams, officers, managers, coaches, umpires, or players may be suspended by OBA for knowingly: Permitting betting or improper conduct by players or officers at games. Offering, agreeing, conspiring, or attempting to lose any game or being interested in any pool or wager thereon. Playing with a team, Direct Entry, Local Association, or Affiliated Association that has been suspended or expelled. Having on its list of signed players any disqualified players. Permitting officers to conduct business of Direct Entries, Local Associations, Affiliated Associations or Associate Members against the best interests of baseball. Violating any OBA policy or Code of Conduct. Any team or player which shall play under an assumed name shall be suspended from OBA and any coach or manager knowingly permitting a player to play under an assumed name shall be suspended from OBA, subject to appeal. Any player found guilty of playing over the age limit permitted in the series in which he/she is participating shall automatically be suspended along with the manager of the team. Any team found guilty of playing a player over the age limit permitted in the series in which he/she is participating, shall automatically be suspended along with the manager of the team. Any manager or coach using an ineligible player in a Tournament, or a Play-off shall be automatically suspended indefinitely. Any further penalty shall be decided by OBA. Any player, coach or manager making physical contact with an umpire will be immediately suspended indefinitely, subject to appeal. Suspensions issued by the Select Division that are communicated to the Rep Division, will be automatically accepted by the Rep Division, and vice versa. Any person suspended by the either division may appeal such suspension as outlined by the OBA Discipline Policy. Appeals will not stay the execution of the suspension and suspended persons may not participate while their appeal is being heard. Any suspension of 2 games or less is not appealable. [TOP] A5.02 Penalties The following Progressive Penalties shall apply at all levels of baseball on minor offenses/infractions by a coach or manager: On a first minor violation, the coach or manager shall receive a warning from the umpire. If the coach/manager continues after being warned, or on any second minor violation in that game, the umpire shall order the coach/manager be restricted to the dugout for the remainder of the game. On any subsequent minor violation in that game, or refusal to remain in the dugout after restriction, the umpire shall eject the coach/manager from the game. The umpire in his/her sole discretion may elect to eject any coach, manager, or player from the game without warning or progressive penalties. Any player, coach or manager ejected by an umpire from any OBA game shall be assessed an automatic one-game which is to be the next scheduled game of consequence (ie: not exhibition games). The player, coach or manager may be subject to further suspension depending on the severity of the circumstances leading to the ejection at the discretion of the tournament host’s protest and discipline committee. The umpires, who umpire a game where an ejection occurs, shall file an ejection report with the convenor of the tournament within 2 days of the completion of the tournament. Failure to submit an ejection report shall result in the suspension of the umpire(s) until the report is filed. [TOP] A5.03 Commissioner Appeals Any decisions made by the Rep or Select Division (other than a protested game, classifications or appeal of fines) may be appealed to the appropriate Commissioner, in writing, with a deposit of $100.00. The appeal must be made within 14 days of the decision being appealed. When the Commissioner receives an appeal in writing, the Commissioner shall review the appeal and determine whether, in the Commissioner’s discretion, the appeal either presents new information not available or presented to the board or committee which made the decision appealed from, or represents an important and compelling matter which justifies the holding of an appeal. Where the Commissioner determines that either (i) or (ii) applies, the Commissioner shall name a committee of at least 3 members of the appropriate Council who shall hear the appeal. The committee shall also rule on the disposition of the deposit. The Commissioner shall establish the time and place of the appeal hearing. The hearing must be held within 45 days of receiving the written appeal. The 45 days shall begin the day following the receipt of the written appeal. The appellant must be present in person at the hearing. Failure to appear will result in a default decision and no further appeal shall be allowed. A decision resulting from a Commissioner’s Appeal may be appealed to the OBA President. The President will have the discretion to refer the appeal to the OBA Dispute Resolution Committee, or to address the appeal through the President’s Appeal Procedure described below. Decisions, which are disciplinary in nature, made by a Local Association or Affiliated Association may be appealed by a member of the Local Association or Affiliated Association using the Commissioner's Appeal Process. The Commissioner will apply only the criteria in (b)(ii) above to determine if a hearing will be granted. [TOP] A5.04 President's Appeal When the President receives an appeal in writing, the President shall review the appeal and determine whether, in the President’s discretion, the appeal either: presents new information not available or presented to the board or committee which made the decision appealed from, or represents an important and compelling matter which justifies the holding of an appeal. Where the President determines that either (i) or (ii) applies, the President shall name a committee of at least 3 members of the OBA Board of Management who shall hear the appeal. The decision of the committee shall be final. The committee shall also rule on the disposition of the deposit. The decision of the committee on the appeal may increase, decrease or leave alone any sanction or penalty awarded as part of the decision being appealed. The President shall establish the time and place of the appeal hearing. The hearing must be held within 45 days of receiving the written appeal. The 45 days shall begin the day following the receipt of the written appeal. The appellant must be present in person at the hearing. Failure to appear will result in a default decision and no further appeal shall be allowed. Decisions of the OBA (other than decisions of the Rep Division or Select Division) may be appealed to the OBA Dispute Resolution Committee. Decisions resulting from a Dispute Resolution Committee appeal, are eligible to be appealed to the President using the President’s Appeal process described starting in paragraph a) above. [TOP] A5.05 Appeal of Fines Any Direct Entry, Local Association, or Affiliated Association desiring to appeal a fine imposed upon a team, a Direct Entry, a Local Association, or an Affiliated Association must file a request to appeal the fine with OBA within 45 days of receipt of notification of the fine. (Any fine issued by the Rep Division with respect to a tournament must be issued within 30 days of receipt of the Convenor’s Report.) A panel established by the OBA Board of Directors, or their designate, must hear an appeal of a fine within 45 days after the appeal is filed with the OBA Office. [TOP] A6.01 Reporting Abuse Any incident that is considered to be verbal or physical abuse of or by any participants in any sanctioned event shall be reported by the event supervisor(s), tournament convenor(s), OBA officials and/or the umpire(s) to the OBA Office. [TOP] A7.01 Championship Awards Championship teams of each Play-off series shall receive an award representative of the championship. The OBA will present the following numbers of awards to members of the championship team and finalist team of each classification: All Select Championships: 18 awards Rep Championships: 15U and below: 20 awards Rep Championships: 16U and 18U: 20 awards Rep Championships: 22U, Senior and Women’s: 25 awards [TOP] A8.01 Coaches and Managers To be on a roster after June 30th of the playing year, coaches and field managers must meet the certification requirements as set out by OBA. OBA shall publish a list of the certification requirements by the AGM annually. For Rep Elimination tournaments, coaches must also meet the Baseball Canada certification requirements applicable to the national tournament they would attend if their team won the Eliminations. Managers and coaches will be responsible for the conduct of their players both on and off the field until departure from the park. An infraction may result in a suspension for the next scheduled game in that OBA Play‐off or to further suspension depending on the severity of the circumstances of the incident at the discretion of the tournament host’s protest and discipline committee. Nothing in this paragraph limits the application of A5.01 or A5.02. In 22U and below, at least one certified coach or manager listed in the Helpers Roster of the Local Association must be present and on the bench for each game. If there is no coach/manager present or the only coach/manager present is ejected, then the game is forfeited. All coaches and managers come under the jurisdiction of the umpires during games and are subject to penalties. The umpires shall see that the rules governing coaches and managers in all games are strictly enforced. As per the Official Rules of Baseball, in 14U and above, any team member in uniform may act as a base coach. Where a player in 14U, 15U, 16U or 18U is acting as a base coach, the player must wear a helmet. [TOP] A9.01 Umpires - General The OBA Umpire Supervisor shall determine annually the umpires to be certified with the OBA. The umpire is to pay a certification fee plus complete a certification process as determined by the OBA and outlined on the OBA website. The website will be updated annually by February 1st with the requirements for that year. For all OBA Championships (Rep and Select), umpires must complete an umpire report, available on the OBA website, to the OBA Office concerning ejections and protests. Reports must be completed for each game involving ejections and/or protests within 24 hours of the scheduled game. All umpires officiating OBA-sanctioned games in Ontario must hold active certification with the OBA or another OBA-approved Provincial Sport Organization. This requirement applies to all OBA-sanctioned baseball in Ontario, including but not limited to Rep and Select league and exhibition play, house league play, tournament play, OBA Championships, and National Qualifiers. Any Local Association or Affiliated Association that assigns or permits an uncertified umpire to officiate an OBA-sanctioned game shall be fined $500 per incident, and game results shall stand. There shall be no fines issued for house league infractions for the 2026 season. The OBA may impose additional sanctions as deemed appropriate in cases of repeat offences. The result of games played with uncertified umpires shall stand, the insurance coverage for all participants shall be unaffected, and the certification of an umpire is not subject to in-game protest. Incidents of alleged infractions should be reported to the OBA office. The home team in a home and home style Play-off shall notify, supply, and pay for official OBA umpires. All umpires are to be classified as neutral. A list of active OBA certified umpires will be made available on the OBA website. All certifications are to be renewed yearly. Umpires handling OBA games must have current OBA certification. An umpire may not officiate any OBA game involving a team of which the umpire is a member. All umpires shall be properly dressed as specified by the OBA Umpires Committee when officiating at any baseball games involving OBA teams. Failure to comply could mean loss of accreditation. In all final series of a home and home style Play-off, 3 umpires will officiate with the exception of the 10U, 11U, 12U and 13U where 2 umpires will suffice. In tournament style OBA Play-offs 2 umpires will suffice except for the final game(s) at 14U, 15U, 16U Girls, 16U, 18U, 21U, Women’s and Senior where there will be 3 umpires. 2 umpires will suffice for the final game(s) at T-Ball, 9U, 10U, 11U, 12U and 13U. [TOP] A9.02 Umpire Fees No umpires will be paid by the OBA. The home team in each game of a home and home style OBA Play-off must pay the umpires’ fees. [TOP] A10.01 Double First Base (Safety Base) Starting in 2027, for all sanctioned play at 11U and below, a double first base (safety base) shall be required. The double first base shall measure 15” x 30”. One half (white) shall be placed in fair territory adjoining first base, and the other half (orange or contrasting colour) shall be placed in foul territory Use on Batted Balls – The defensive player making a play on a batted ball at first base must use the white (fair territory) portion of the base. The batter-runner must use the orange (foul territory) portion of the base when running through first base. Use on Subsequent Plays – On any play where the runner is attempting to advance to second base, or when returning to first base, the white portion of the base shall be used. If a play is being made from foul territory (e.g. dropped third strike, bunt, or overthrow), the batter-runner may use either portion of the base to avoid collision. Effect of Violation – If the fielder uses only the orange portion of the base to attempt to record a put-out on a batted ball, the runner shall be declared safe. If the runner uses the white portion of the base on a batted ball and, in the judgment of the umpire, interferes with the defensive player, the runner shall be declared out for interference. A11.01 Bat Rules The maximum legal diameter for bats shall be 2 3/4". Subject to the following, in addition to wooden bats, metal bats shall be allowed in OBA play. For all 12U and 13U play, metal bats with (i) a 1.15 BPF (Bat Performance Factor) or (ii) USA Baseball Model is permitted. For this division using up to a minus 10 length/weight differential is permitted. For 14U and 15U play, wood bats and non-wood bats may be used. Only non-wood bats with a length-to-weight differential of -5 or less are permitted. For all levels of play 16U and above, only wood, wood composite, and wood-like material bats are permitted. Wood composite bats include wood or bamboo bats with fiberglass sheathing and wood-barrelled bats with composite handles. Wood composite bats do not include any bat that has any metallic component. All players must use the approved bats for the series in which they are playing regardless of age or gender. [TOP] A12.01 Approved Baseballs Each year, the OBA will approve a list of baseballs for use in the upcoming season. MAKE MODEL MANUFACTURER'S RECOMMENDED USE Rawlings TVB/TVB850 Indoor/Rally Cap ROTB1/ROTB5 Indoor/8U/9U 45CC Up to 9U RTDC/RTD1/ROLB1X Up to 13U 65CC 10U to 15U 80CC 10U to 18U ROMLCAN* All divisions ROMC All divisions Louisville Slugger Canada BB93 T-Ball BB11 10U to 13U BB12 14U to Senior BB12-RL 16U to Senior Mizuno MB380 12U to Senior MB380FS 12U to Senior Silverstar Sports 818LS 8U-15U DUBS Baseball DB300 10U - 18U DB100 All divisions Rawlings is the official baseball of OBA. *Rawlings ROMLCAN is the exclusive baseball of OBA Elimination Tournaments. [TOP] A13.01 Arm Care Rules Unless otherwise stated, all Arm Care rules listed in A12.01 shall be in force throughout the baseball season and shall apply to all league play and tournament play as sanctioned by OBA or any Affiliated Association. This includes games played outside Ontario and outside Canada, regardless of the tournament’s pitch count rules or lack thereof. Teams attending Baseball Canada National Championships are not required to follow these rules to the extent that they are following arm care rules as outlined by Baseball Canada. Unless otherwise stated, all Arm Care rules listed in A12.01 shall be enforced at all series 22U and below. Players not listed on a team’s pitch count app prior to the start of the game are ineligible to pitch during that game. Call-up players can be added to the app’s roster prior to the first pitch of the game. The Official Pitch Counter for that game is responsible for advising the umpire if a player who is not in the app for that team enters the game to warm up as a pitcher prior to a game-pitch being thrown. If a game-pitch is thrown by an ineligible pitcher, the penalties listed in section A12.02 e. shall apply. An ineligible pitcher shall be removed from the pitching position immediately, but may assume another position other than catcher. Pitchers shall be limited to the number of pitches thrown in a single day as outlined below. Pitchers are required to rest in accordance with the chart below as determined by the total number of pitches thrown on any one calendar day. 9U* 11U 13U 15U 18U 22U REST REQUIRED 1‐20 1‐25 1‐30 1‐35 1‐40 1‐45 None 21‐35 26‐40 31‐45 36‐50 41‐55 46‐60 1 Day 36‐50 41‐55 46‐60 51‐65 56‐70 61‐75 2 Days N/A 56‐65 61‐75 66‐80 71‐85 76‐90 3 Days N/A 66‐75 76‐85 81‐95 86‐105 91‐115 4 Days 50 75 85 95 105 115 Daily Max *Please see A12.01(n) for 9U player pitching requirements. Note: Please see A12.01(h) for multiple games in any two consecutive days. For the purposes calculating days of rest required, the day after game day is considered Day 1 of the rest period. (ie: If an 11U pitcher throws 27 pitches on Friday, they will be next eligible to pitch again on Sunday). If a game extends beyond midnight into the next calendar day, any pitches thrown thereafter shall be counted toward the pitch count of the day the game started. If, however, the game is suspended for any reason after midnight, when it resumes, any pitches thrown by any pitcher shall be counted toward the new day. Intentional walks and balks shall count toward the pitcher’s pitch count total if a pitch has been thrown. Players are permitted to pitch in 2 separate games in the same day as long as they do not exceed the maximum number of pitches that requires no rest for their age category in the first game of the day. (ie: If an 11U pitcher throws fewer than 25 pitches in the first game, the player may pitch in a second game.) No player is permitted to pitch in more than 2 games in any two-day period regardless of the number of pitches thrown. No player may exceed the Daily Maximum for their age category in any two‐day period. If a player pitches in two games in a day or on two consecutive days, days rest required shall be calculated using the combined total of the number of pitches thrown. Ex1: 22U player throws 45 pitches on Friday in a tournament (no rest required). That pitcher is permitted to pitch again on Saturday to a maximum of 70 pitches so as not to exceed 115. Ex2: In the same scenario as Ex1, the total number of pitches thrown are added together to determine days rest. If the player threw 45 pitches on Friday and 70 pitches on Saturday, the pitcher must rest for 4 days and is next eligible to pitch on Thursday. Pitchers will be permitted to complete the at‐bat of the batter they are facing when their Daily Maximum pitch limit has been reached but must be removed immediately after the batter is retired, or the batter becomes a runner, or the third out of the inning is made. When a pitcher reaches the maximum number of pitches allowed for any days-rest threshold, they may complete the at-bat without affecting their days rest, as long as they do not pitch to another batter. In this situation, the number of pitches corresponding to the threshold will be indicated on the pitching log (not the actual pitch count). The coach or manager must acknowledge this with the umpire prior to the threshold pitch being thrown. The umpire would then notify the official scorekeeper. PENALTY: If the coach or manager does not inform the umpire prior to the pitcher reaching the threshold, the prescribed days of rest as outlined A12.01(d) will be upheld based on the actual number of pitches thrown. Example: The 15U threshold for zero days rest is 35 pitches. The pitcher begins the new batter at 32 pitches. Prior to the 35th pitch, the Manager indicates that this shall be the last batter for the pitcher. The pitcher is permitted to complete the current batter and a final pitch count of 35 is recorded. The total number of pitches thrown by an ambidextrous pitcher shall be charged toward their pitch count regardless of which arm(s), or combination thereof, the pitcher uses to throw. No player may pitch on three consecutive days unless their total pitch count for the three days does not exceed the number of pitches where no rest is required for that age category. A pitcher may not pitch on 4 consecutive days regardless of pitch count. Once a player assumes the pitching position, they are no longer eligible to catch for the remainder of the day. Once a player assumes the catcher position, they are no longer eligible to pitch for the remainder of the day. Any player who is playing in a series higher than their age-appropriate series shall be restricted to the pitch count limitations and required rest periods applicable to the age of the player, provided the following: Players aged 8 and under and not permitted to pitch in any circumstance. 9-year-old players who are called‐up to play on a 10U or 11U team are not permitted to pitch. 9-year-old players who are rostered to a 10U or 11U team shall be permitted to pitch with the following limitations: 0 Days Rest: 1‐20 Pitches 1 Day Rest: 21‐35 Pitches 2 Days Rest: 36‐50 Pitches Daily Maximum: 50 Pitches Any player who is playing in a series higher than their age must be identified on the lineup card. Any underage player not identified on the lineup card is ineligible to pitch. Should any such unidentified underage player pitch, the pitcher shall be removed from the pitching position and the Manager ejected. When a player reaches their Daily Maximum pitch count, the manager or coach may remove the pitcher from the pitching position immediately without penalty, regardless of the number of trips already charged to the pitcher during the inning or at‐bat. For all games except tournament play, the home team shall designate an official pitch counter for their team who shall be responsible for tracking the pitches of both teams. For all games except tournament play, the Manager (or designate) of the home team shall record the number of pitches thrown for all of both teams' players within 24 hours (or before the start of the next game, whichever is earlier) of the game’s start time using the OBA Pitch Count Tracker. For all OBA sanctioned tournament play, (including Provincial Championships, and Elimination Tournaments), the Home team is responsible for capturing the pitch count in the pitch count app in real-time (either pitch by pitch or at the end of each inning) during the game. Failure to complete this in real-time will result in the game being forfeited to the Away team. Should the game not be captured in real-time as a result of negligence and it is not realized until after the game, the game will not be forfeited but will result in a one-game suspension for the Head Coach. Exception: where cell service is weak or down and the tournament convenor gives permission, games may be entered post-game within 20 minutes following the end of the game. Notwithstanding A12.01(r), in all OBA Tournament play, the tournament host may choose to appoint an official pitch counter for the game at their discretion. If a host chooses this option, and the pitch count is not entered in real-time during the game, the home team will not be penalized. A $1000 fine will apply to the host. The official pitch counter must provide the current pitch count upon request by either manager or the umpire. The official pitch counter shall inform the plate umpire when a pitcher has delivered their Daily Maximum pitch count. The plate umpire shall inform the manager that the pitcher must be removed, (i) immediately if the Daily Maximum threshold has been reached at the end of an at‐bat, or (ii) following the completion of the at‐bat or inning, subject to A12.01(i). Note: Failure by the Official Pitch Counter to inform the plate umpire of a pitcher reaching their Daily Maximum pitch count does not relieve the Manager of their responsibility to know the pitch count and remove the pitcher. Penalty: Failure to remove the pitcher with no notification by the Official Pitch Counter will result in the manager’s immediate ejection and the pitcher shall be removed from the pitching position immediately and will not be permitted to complete the at‐bat. This will be treated as an emergency situation and the incoming pitcher shall receive as many warm‐up pitches as the plate umpire deems necessary. The Manager of the opposing team will receive notification of the pitch count entry into the system and will have 24 hours from that time to verify or challenge the pitch count from that game. Any challenge of an official pitch count will be forwarded to the Series Chair for resolution. The decision of the Series Chair with regard to Pitch Counts shall be final. A13.02 Arm Care Penalties For violation of any Arm Care rules as prescribed in A12.01 (c) to (m), The Head Coach is ejected, and the pitcher is immediately removed from the pitching position. The Head Coach shall receive a five (5) game suspension. The game will continue. If the official pitch counter does not make the Head Coach and Umpire aware of the violation until after the game, the Head Coach will still be deemed to be ejected and suspended. If a pitcher throws during their mandatory days rest period in violation of A12.01(c), the pitcher’s maximum days rest period will reset to the maximum rest period of 4 days from the date of the appearance constituting the violation. In the event that a player pitches on a required rest day and the violation is not caught until after the game, the player shall be required to rest 4 complete days from the day they last pitched, and the Head Coach will receive an automatic two-game suspension to be served at the next scheduled game of consequence. In any case, if the arm care violation is not caught until after the game, the game will not be retroactively forfeited. Failure to enter pitch count results within the specified timelines, as set forth in A12.01(q) and (r), will result in the following penalties: On a team’s first offense of the season, immediate suspension of the Head Coach until the pitch count is entered. On a team’s second (or any subsequent offense), the Head Coach will receive an automatic 5-game suspension. Any coach or Head Coach whose actions cause a player’s pitch counts to be exceeded or not counted as required by these rules, and that action is not identified until after the game is completed, shall be subject to a suspension of not less than 2 games which may be extended at the discretion of the Series Chair. If a team has no pitchers eligible to pitch in that game, the game is over and the team forfeits. A final score of 7-0 and 7 defensive innings will be recorded for the winning team. The following chart shall be used to enforce Arm Care violations: TEAM VIOLATION VIOLATION REPORTED PENALTY Home Team violates pitch count In game Forfeit + 1 game suspension for Head Coach Home Team violates pitch count Post-game No forfeit 2 game suspension for Head Coach Away Team violates pitch count after notification by Official Pitch Counter to umpire at time of infraction In game Forfeit + 1 game suspension for Head Coach Away Team violates pitch count with NO notification to umpire prior to the time of infraction In game No forfeit Head Coach is ejected + 1 game suspension for Head Coach Away team violates pitch count Post-game No forfeit 2 game suspension for Head Coach IMPORTANT NOTE ON ENFORCEMENT: The intent of these rules is to enforce the pitch count rules and not to promote forfeits. The Home Team is responsible for the pitch count and therefore the duty of responsibility falls upon them to notify the opposing team of a pitcher reaching their pitch count via the plate umpire. Regardless of whether or not the Home Team Head Coach delegates the pitch count to someone else, they are ultimately responsible and accountable for the Pitch Count. [TOP] A14.01 Female Baseball Only female players (including those identifying as female as per the Equity, Diversity, and Inclusion Policy) are eligible to play Girls Baseball. A female may, but is not required to, register with both a Girls Baseball team and a non-Girls Baseball team. Where the Local Association in whose territory the female lives offers both Girls Baseball and non-Girls Baseball teams, the female may register with either a Girls Baseball team or a non-Girls Baseball team or both in such Local Association and on the basis of such registration play on any team from such Local Association for which she is eligible to play based upon her age. Where the Local Association in which the female lives does not offer Girls Baseball, the female may register for non-Girls Baseball with such Local Association and Girls Baseball with another Local Association without a release being required from the Local Association in whose territory the female lives. Except as provided for in this clause (i), a female Rep player requires a release in accordance with the provisions of section RP1.5 in the same manner as a male player. Female athletes have the option of playing one-year overage in all divisions. Rep-rostered female players are allowed to participate on a Select team. PSWA (provincial Women’s Softball Associations) carded players are eligible to participate on a Rep or Select team. [TOP] A15.01 Playing Non-Recognized Programs At the OBA level, the Affiliated Association Level and the Local Association Level, teams that play against non-recognized baseball program(s) as defined in RP2.11 d) i), of the Rep Division Playing Rules shall not be penalized for doing so. This applies to both Rep Division and Select Division. [TOP] A16.01 Field Dimensions Regulation Field Diagrams [TOP] A17.01 Amendments Amendments to these OBA Procedures and Playing Rules may be proposed by: the Board of Directors; or the Rep Council; or the Select Council; or an Affiliated Association in good standing; or a Loop Association in good standing; or a Select community baseball association in good standing. Rep Commissioner in consultation with Deputy Rep Commissioner or vice versa. Proposed amendments must be submitted at the OBA Office by a deadline set by the Board. Proposed amendments to sections A1.01 through A6.01, and A15.01 shall be approved by Members as follows: All Rep Members, Select Members, Board Members and Local Members shall have the right to vote with the voting points assigned to them in the OBA By-laws. Changes will be approved by a simple majority of eligible voters present. Proposed amendments to sections A6.01 through A14.01 can be approved by Board of Directors. title: Oba Pitch Count App User Guide
snippet: Skip to main content OBA Pitch Count App User Guide – Arm Care COACHING CENTRE UMPIRE ZONE FEMALE BASEBALL ARM CARE MORE SIGN IN / REGISTER OBA Pitch Count App User Guide Updated for the 2026 baseball season OBA Pitch Count App User Guide The OBA Pitch Count A
searchText: Oba Pitch Count App User Guide OBA Pitch Count App User Guide Skip to main content OBA Pitch Count App User Guide – Arm Care COACHING CENTRE UMPIRE ZONE FEMALE BASEBALL ARM CARE MORE SIGN IN / REGISTER OBA Pitch Count App User Guide Updated for the 2026 baseball season OBA Pitch Count App User Guide The OBA Pitch Count App is provided free for all OBA teams. Only Coaches and rostered staff are permitted access to the full functionality of the app, such as entering games, adding players, and adding pitch counts. Coaches may delegate access to a volunteer to enter pitch counts only. In order for coaches to access the app, you must be on an approved OBA roster with a valid and unique email address and your NCCP number. Two coaches cannot share the same email address in the system. Please email pitchcountapp@baseballontario.com for support. Please note this email address is not monitored 24/7, your email will be responded to as soon as possible. This user guide is intended to provide information on how to use the app and any mention to the pitch count rules are for reference purposes only and should not be considered the actual rule. Please see the Playing Rules for full text of the rules. IMPORTANT NOTE: The Home Team must enter the pitch counts for both teams. Do not enter multiple entries for the same game (ie: one game for the away team and one game for the home team). Both teams' pitch counts must be entered as one game by the home team. Download the App Pitch Count App User Guide Adding a New Game Adding Pitch Counts Live-in-Game Adding Pitch Counts Post-Game Adding a Call-Up Player to a Team Approving or Challenging Pitch Counts Change Uniform Number Checking Pitcher Availability Deleting a Game Deleting Pitch Counts Editing After Approval (Reopen) Edit Game Details Edit Score After Approval Favourite Teams Game Statuses No Opponent Games (Out of Province Tournaments/High School/Ad Hoc) Removing a Pitcher from a Game Registration to Pitch Count App Signing in to the App Statistics Additional How-To Series Troubleshooting Login & Access Issues Pitch Count Support ADDING A NEW GAME From the main screen of Pitch Count, tap your team name under My Teams. Select the Games tab and tap ⊕ ADD GAME Enter the Game Date and Game Time. Select the Visiting Team. Pro Tip: You can search for the correct team by team number, team name, or filter by division). Add the game Location. Tap Save. Remember: Home Team must enter pitch counts for both teams. [TOP] ADDING PITCH COUNTS, LIVE IN-GAME Capturing pitch counts live-in-game is the preferred method of counting pitches. This eliminates the risk of pitchers exceeding pitch counts, using pitchers who are in recovery, and alerts both coaches to potential rule violations during the game. From the Games tab, select the game you wish to count pitches for. Tap . BEGIN PITCH COUNT . Select the pitcher you wish to start the Top of the 1st inning. You may be asked to enter the pitcher's jersey number, enter that here (you can change it later) and tap Update, otherwise Skip. Tap the large blue + sign to add pitches as they are delivered. Pro Tip: All pitches delivered to the batter must be counted as a pitch, this includes balks that are thrown to the batter. A balk that is thrown to a base, or a balk that is not thrown is not counted. The app will display in large numbers how many pitches have been thrown this inning by this pitcher. This will turn amber when the pitcher has 10 pitches left until they hit their daily maximum and red when they hit their daily maximum. Above the pitches thrown this inning, the app displays a countdown of how many pitches this pitcher has remaining today. This will turn amber when the pitcher has 10 pitches left until they hit their daily maximum and red when they hit their daily maximum. You can edit how many pitches have been thrown in the inning by selecting the pencil icon above the pitches remaining today. Tap . Next . to select a relief pitcher, or to move on to the next half-inning. When the game is complete, tap the back button on your device to go back to the main screen for the game. Tap . END GAME AND REQUEST APPROVAL . to finalize the game. Note: When you request approval, the game is locked and requires verification from the visiting team. [TOP] ADDING PITCH COUNTS, POST GAME If you are unable to capture pitch counts live-in-game using the app, pitch counts may be captured on paper and entered in the app post-game. From the Games tab, select the game you wish to count pitches for. Tap . ADD TOTAL PITCH COUNT . Select the first team you wish to add pitches for. Select the pitcher you wish to add pitches for. Enter the total number of pitches thrown by that pitcher. Tap UPDATE Repeat steps 2-6 until all pitches have been entered for both teams. When the game is complete, tap the back button on your device to go back to the main screen for the game. Tap . END GAME AND REQUEST APPROVAL . to finalize the game. Note: When you request approval, the game is locked and requires verification from the visiting team. [TOP] ADDING A CALL-UP PLAYER TO A TEAM From the Roster tab, tap ⊕ ADD PLAYER Search the player's first and last name in the system. Note: The player you wish to add must be on an approved Baseball Ontario roster uploaded into the Pitch Count database already in order to add them to a team. Players matching the name searched will populate in the Search Results screen with the team name that they are a full-time roster player on. Select the correct player from the list. A confirmation pop-up will appear, tap ADD PLAYER if you wish to add this player to your team. [TOP] APPROVING OR CHALLENGING PITCH COUNTS Once the Home Team has submitted a pitch count for approval, the Visiting Team must verify the pitch count is accurate within 5 days of submission. All coaches on the team will receive a notification that a game approval has been requested. Log in to the Pitch Count app. Approval Requests will display at the top of the app, tap the game you wish to verify. Verify that all pitch counts have been entered correctly. Tap . APPROVE PITCH COUNTS . if the pitch counts were entered correctly. Tap . CHALLENGE PITCH COUNTS . if there are corrections that need to be made on the pitch counts. When you challenge the pitch count, you must enter a message to the Home Team coach why you are challenging the pitch count. The game status will change to CHALLENGED and the Home Team can then edit the pitch counts and resubmit for approval. Pro Tip: If a game is not verified, the app will auto-verify the game after 2 days. [TOP] CHANGE UNIFORM NUMBER Log in to the Pitch Count app. Select the team you wish to edit under the MY TEAMS header. Select the ROSTER tab at the top. Android: Tap the ellipsis (three vertical dots) to the right of the player's name on the player card and tap Edit. Enter the new uniform number and tap UPDATE. Apple: Swipe left on the player card that you wish to edit and tap Edit. Enter the uniform number and tap Save. [TOP] CHECKING PITCHER AVAILABILITY From the main screen, select your team under the MY TEAMS header. Select the ROSTER tab. Each player on the roster will have a player card displayed on this tab. The player's jersey number, if entered, is displayed in the circle next to the player's name. The number of pitches thrown on the pitcher's last outing and the date they last threw will appear on this card. If the pitcher has any pitches remaining today, they will also appear on this card. Pitcher's statuses are colour-coded to help identify if a player is able to pitch today: Available - Pitcher is available to pitch today. In Recovery - Pitcher is not available to pitch today. The next available date the pitcher is available to pitch will display on the card. [TOP] DELETING A GAME For a game to be deleted, there can be no pitcher attached to the game, please see Deleting Pitch Counts to remove all pitchers from the game prior to deleting the game. Ensure all pitchers have been removed from the game. From the games screen select the game you wish to delete. Android: Tap the pencil icon in the top right corner of the game screen and select . DELETE . Apple: Tap the Edit button in the top right corner of the game screen and select Remove Game [TOP] DELETING PITCH COUNTS Pitch Counts for pitchers who have pitches in a game can be removed if they have been entered in error. If a game has been entered in error (or in duplicate), this process must be completed for all pitchers before the game can be deleted. Note: This process can only be completed for games that are In Progress, Challenged, or Reopened status. Deleting entire pitch counts for an inning for a pitcher can be done within the game screen. Android: Tap the ellipsis (three vertical dots) next to the pitch count you wish to delete and tap delete. Apple: Swipe left over the player's pitch count you wish to delete and tap . Delete . On the confirmation screen, confirm you want to delete the pitch count for this pitcher. [TOP] EDITING A GAME'S PITCH COUNTS AFTER SUBMITTING FOR APPROVAL (Reopen) A game can be edited after it has been approved if has been discovered that the pitch counts are incorrect. Either team can initiate this process, but only the Home Team can edit the pitch counts once the game has been re-opened. Only games that have been verified can be reopened with this process. To initiate the reopening of the game: From the Games screen, tap the verified game you wish to reopen. Tap . REQUEST TO REOPEN GAME . Enter a message to the opposing coach to request the game to be reopened. Tap Request to Reopen The opposing coach will receive a notification that the game has been requested to be reopened. The opposing coach must then open the app and choose either Reopen Game or Deny Reopen Request. If the game is reopened, the Home Team coach can edit the pitch counts and resubmit for approval. If the reopen request is denied, the game will automatically revert to Verified status. [TOP] EDIT GAME DETAILS You can change the date, time, game type, and location of a game without deleting the pitch counts but only while the game is in a Pending status. In order to change the date, time, or opponent of a game that is already submitted, you must challenge the game and then all pitch counts already entered in for that game must first be deleted. Please see Deleting Pitch Counts to remove all pitchers from the game prior to changing the game. Ensure all pitchers have been removed from the game. From the games screen select the game you wish to edit. Android: Tap the pencil icon in the top right corner of the game screen and select the field you wish to edit (ie: date, time, Home Team, or Away Team). Tap SAVE. Apple: Tap the Edit button in the top right corner of the game screen and select the field you wish to edit (ie: date, time, Home Team, or Away Team). Tap SAVE. [TOP] EDIT SCORE AFTER APPROVAL To edit scores after the game has been submitted for approval, you must wait for the game to be approved (or approve the game if you are the approver) and then you can request for it to be reopened. Request to reopen the game. Enter "Incorrect game score" in the details. The other coach must approve the reopen request. Once reopened, only the Home Team coach can tap . END GAME AND REQUEST APPROVAL . Change the score and defensive innings. Tap . END GAME AND REQUEST APPROVAL . The Away Team must now re-approve the game. [TOP] FAVOURITE TEAMS Adding Favourite Teams to the Pitch Count app will allow you to quickly select teams you play against more frequently or during tournaments. Search for the team using the search button or one of the filter options. Select the team you wish to make a favourite. Tap the Roster tab to verify the players on the team are correct to ensure you have the correct team. Tap the star icon in the top right corner of the app. [TOP] GAME STATUS STATUS DESCRIPTION No Status Games with no status have not yet been started. In Progress Games with In Progress status have been started but have not yet been submitted for approval. Approval Requested Games with Approval Requested Status have been submitted to the opponent for approval but have not yet been challenged or approved. Challenged Game was submitted by the Home Team but the Visiting Coach challenged the pitch count. Home Team must edit and/or re-submit. Requested to Reopen Game was verified but one of the teams requested the game to be reopened and edited. Reopened Request to reopen the game was approved and editing is in progress. Verified Game has been approved and pitch counts verified. [TOP] NO OPPONENT GAMES Out of Province Tournaments, High School Games, and other Ad Hoc Appearances All pitching appearances for OBA rostered pitchers should be captured in the Pitch Count app, including if the player plays on a non-OBA team (ie: High School, IBL, Senior, etc). To do this, the coach of the player's team should create a game against no opponent and enter the pitcher's total pitch count for the game. The final score can be entered as 0-0 and the defensive innings as 1 inning each. Home Team: Your team Away Team: Do not select another team, leave this blank Game Type: Exhibition Game Location: High School, US Tournament, Ad Hoc, etc. For High School or college games, the app will require you to enter a catcher in order to close the game. Select the same pitcher as your catcher. This will generate an rule violation - that is expected. When the game is submitted for approval, it will be auto-verified by the system after 2 days. Similarly, if a 22U or 18U team is playing a Senior team, the 22U/18U team would enter no opponent because Senior teams do not have a pitch count limitation. In this case, enter the final score and defensive innings correctly. Note: This is the only situation where it is permissible for only one team to enter a pitch count. [TOP] PITCH COUNT APP REGISTRATION Only coaches and staff on a current OBA approved roster can access the Pitch Count app's counting tools. Ensure your coaches and staff email addresses are valid and accurate on the OBA roster submission form. Ensure your coaches are listed as either Head Coach or Assistant Coach on the roster. Ensure your coaches have their NCCP number listed. Ensure staff are listed as Pitch Counter. Submit your roster to OBA. Await approval from Affiliated Association. Once approved, your roster will be uploaded into the Pitch Count app and you will receive an email from Baseball Ontario Support with the subject Welcome to Baseball Ontario. Download the app from the Google Play Store or Apple App Store before you tap the Join button. Tap the join button. Create a password for the app. Sign into the app using the login credentials you just created. Pro Tip: Once you have signed in to the app, if you are unable to see My Teams you are likely signed in as a Guest. Sign out and sign back in. [TOP] SIGNING IN TO THE APP Depending on your access, you can sign into the app in one of two ways: Coaches: Sign into the app using the credentials created through the rostering process (see Registration to Pitch Count App). Players/Parents/Guests: Tap . ENTER AS GUEST . on the sign in screen to enter the app as a guest. You can search for your favourite teams and tap the team you want to follow, then tap the star at the top right to save them as a Favourite. Once you have saved at least one team as a favourite, the app will display My Favorites at the top of the app the next time you come back to the app! [TOP] STATISTICS Pitching Statistics are kept for each player on your team. You can view historical stats showing how many games, innings, and pitches each pitcher has thrown in the last 4 days, 1 week, one month, this season, and all time. From the main screen, tap the team you wish to view stats on. Tap the STATS tab. All stats will display on the screen for each pitcher on your roster. [TOP] TROUBLESHOOTING LOGIN & ACCESS ISSUES Only coaches and staff on an approved OBA roster, submitted through the BaseballShift rostering system will be granted access to the Pitch Count app. Please note, it can take up to 72 hours for rosters to be uploaded to the system. Uploads are not completed on weekends. If you have received your invitation for this year, created your password, and you are able to log in but you are unable to add games and/or approve games, please follow these steps: Uninstall the Pitch Count app (this clears the cache from your device). Reinstall the Pitch Count app but do not log back in yet. Tap the Forgot Password link on the main screen of the Pitch Count app to change your password. Once you have changed your password, log back in to the Pitch Count app using your new credentials. If you are still having issues logging in, please email pitchcountapp@baseballontario.com for assistance. Please note that this email address is not monitored 24/7, your email will be responded to as soon as possible. [TOP] HOW DO I SUBMIT A TROUBLE TICKET FOR THE PITCH COUNT APP? If you encounter a bug, have feedback on the app, or have a question for Pitch Count Support, please email pitchcountapp@baseballontario.com BEFORE YOU EMAIL: Be sure to email from the email address associated with your Pitch Count app if possible. Always include your Team ID which can be found in the URL for your BaseballShift roster (see below). If your question is in regard to a specific game, please include the game number. If your question is in regard to a specific player or coach, please include their full name. Please note that this email address is not monitored 24/7, your email will be responded to as soon as possible. [TOP] Last Updated: 03-June-2024 SearchMember DirectoryEventsEmail Support Copyright © 2026 New Start Mobile, LLC https://ondeck.baseballontario.com/page/784/arm-care/16451/oba-pitch-count-app-user-guide
headings: OBA Pitch Count App User Guide

searchText: Rep Division Playing Rules Rep Division Playing Rules Table of Contents Skip to main content Rep Division Playing Rules – Rep Division Procedures & Playing Rules COACHING CENTRE UMPIRE ZONE FEMALE BASEBALL ARM CARE MORE SIGN IN / REGISTER Rep Division Playing Rules Updated for the 2026 Baseball Season Table of Contents RP1.1 Age and Category RP1.2 Birth Records RP1.3 Residency Rules RP1.4 Players Changing Residence RP1.5 Players Changing Teams, Releases, and Transfers RP1.6 Release Appeals RP1.7 Offenses RP1.8 Advancing to Higher Series RP1.9 Player Registration RP1.10 Team Number RP1.11 Professional Players Registration RP1.12 Eligibility RP1.13 High Performance Programs RP1.14 High Performance Provincial Championships RP1.15 Elite Baseball League of Ontario (EBLO) RP2 Rep Special Playing Rules RP2.1 Uniforms & Equipment RP2.2 Game Length RP2.3 Line-Up Rules RP2.4 Substitutions RP2.5 Pitching Rules RP2.6 Base Running Rules RP2.7 Fielding Rules RP2.8 Extra Inning Rules RP2.9 Protests RP2.10 10U/11U Playing Rules RP2.11 Eligibility RP2.12 Premier Baseball League of Ontario (PBLO) RP5 Rep Provincial Championships RP5.1 Rep Provincial Championship Procedures RP5.2 Tournament Style Rep Provincial Championships RP5.3 Home-and-Home Style Rep Provincial Championships RP5.4 Tournament Format for Rep Provincial Championships RP5.5 Determining the Home Team in Rep Provincial Championships RP6 Rep National Qualifier Tournaments RP6.1 National Qualifier Tournament Procedures RP7.1 Exhibition Games and Out-of-Province Play RP7.2 Open, Invitational, and International Tournaments RP7.3 Tie-Breaker Rules RQ1 Approved Rules of 9U Rep Appendices Appendix A - Guidelines for Affiliated Associations Appendix B - Affiliated Associations Appendix C - National Championship Player Drafts RP1.1 Age and Category To be eligible to play in a particular age category, a player must not have a birthday which makes them older than the number in the series name. ie: A player in 9U (9 and Under) must not turn 10 dururing the current calendar year. CATEGORY/SERIES AGE OF PLAYER AS OF DECEMBER 31st OF THE CALENDAR YEAR 8U Not older than 8 years of age but not younger than 6 as of December 31st 9U Not older than 9 years of age 10U Not older than 10 years of age 11U Not older than 11 years of age 12U Not older than 12 years of age 13U/13U Girls Not older than 13 years of age 14U Not older than 14 years of age 15U Not older than 15 years of age 16U/16U Girls Not older than 16 years of age 18U Not older than 18 years of age 22U Not older than 22 years of age Senior/Women’s Unlimited but not younger than 17 as of December 31st [TOP] RP1.2 Birth Records All players 21 years of age and under must produce a birth record the first time they register with OBA. Only birth certificates, passports, citizenship cards or driver’s licenses will be accepted as birth records. In the case of Affiliated Associations having their own registrar and having the approval of the OBA, it is not necessary to send birth records to the OBA Office if such birth records are provided to the Affiliated Association’s registrar and will be recorded by the registrar. For Affiliated Associations not having their own registrar or for Direct Entries, when sending birth records to the OBA Office, they must be listed, and a copy of the list included with the birth records. Upon receipt of a birth record, it will be recorded in the OBA Office. Players who cannot procure a birth record may sign a team roster upon producing a statutory declaration sworn by their parent or guardian establishing their age; the same to be accompanied by a certificate of a solicitor or notary public. [TOP] RP1.3 Residency Rules The basic premise of the Rep Division’s residence rule is that players are to play with the Local Association where they reside. Players must establish residence within the Local Association on or before June 30th of the playing year. Exception: a player, who (1) maintains Canadian citizenship, (2) has been a member of a Rep Local Association or Direct Entry for at least one year prior, (3) continues to be a member of the same Rep Local Association or Direct Entry, and (4) for any reason is living outside of the Province of Ontario, may continue to register with that Local Association or Direct Entry. Individuals may be granted permission by the Rep Registrar to participate in Rep play. Cases will be dealt with on an individual basis (i.e., exchange students). Affiliated Associations must establish territorial boundaries for Local Associations within the Affiliated Association. Affiliated Associations may also set policies applicable within their Affiliated Association. A copy of the boundaries and any related policies must be filed with Baseball Ontario. The Rep Division will consider the most recent copies filed by the Affiliated Association in any dispute. The Rep Division may, in its discretion, choose to not follow the Affiliated Association policy where it conflicts with the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules and/or the Procedures and Playing Rules of the Rep Division or the best interests of Baseball Ontario. In areas where no Local Association exists, the Affiliated Association may set policy.
